## TreeMap

`/design/data-visualizations/charts/TreeMap/`

The `TreeMap` visualizes the distribution of hierarchical data. It uses nested
rectangles of different sizes and colors to show how much each category or
subcategory contributes to the whole data set.

OverviewProperties

### Import

`tsx
import { TreeMap } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';
`

### Use cases

The `TreeMap` component requires a structured tree object containing
hierarchical data, with an optional `unit` specified as a string. This data
should support two levels of categories: `ClusterNode` and `LeafNode`.

##### Structure

Tree Object: The root of the dataset, containing the overall structure.

- value: A numerical value representing the total value at the root level. - nodes:
An array of cluster nodes. - name: An optional string representing the name of the
root node.

Cluster Nodes: Each parent node represents a category and includes:

- name: A string representing the name of the category. - value: A numerical value
representing the total value of the category. - nodes: An array of leaf nodes.

Leaf Nodes: Each child node represents a sub-category and includes:

- name: A string representing the name of the sub-category. - value: A number representing
the value of the sub-category.

The displayed data can be adjusted by setting limits. These limits can be
defined using the `min` and `max` properties. Setting those limits is
particularly useful when displaying numerical values in the legend
`nameAccessor='value'`

The min property can use the `data-min` value, and the max property can use the
`data-max` value. These special values provide detailed control over the
boundaries.

By default, the data-min value is used for the min property, and the data-max
value is used for the max property. These settings automatically determine the
minimum and maximum values for the nodes in the chart.

Additionally, the min and max properties can also accept numeric values. This
allows you to manually set specific numerical limits for the data display,
giving you even more control over the range of data shown in the chart.

`tsx
{ tree: { name: '', value: 0, nodes: [ { name: 'India', value: 117200000, nodes: [ { name: 'Delhi', value: 27000000, } ] } ] }}
`

Learn more about the data format here.

```tsx
import { TreeMap, TreeMapData } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';

const population: TreeMapData = {
  tree: {
    name: '',
    value: 0,
    nodes: [
      {
        name: 'India',

};

        data={population}
        labelsDisplay="clusters"
        nameAccessor="value"
        min={10_000_000}
        max={20_000_000}
      />
    </>
  );
};
```

#### Size

By default, the chart will use all the available container size up to a maximum
height of 300 pixels. This maximum height can be changed by providing a value in
the `height` prop of the `TreeMap` instance. If a number is passed to this prop
without any unit specified, it will be treated as `px`.

#### Series actions

A series action is a creator-defined interaction with a given node in the chart.
The default interaction included is copying a node name. Inspecting the
underlying data of said node is an example of a basic creator-defined action. In
order to enable chart interactions, the `ChartSeriesAction` subcomponent needs
to be appended within the `TreeMap`. More subcomponents can be added within this
component, for instance `ChartSeriesAction.Item`, where you can provide a custom
action that will appear in the legend menu. That action can execute any custom
logic in its `onSelect` callback or get disabled via a `disabled` prop.
`Intents` could be added as part of the series action as well with
`ChartSeriesAction.Intent`.

```tsx

              </ChartSeriesAction.ItemIcon>
              Select node
            </ChartSeriesAction.Item>
          </ChartSeriesAction>

      />
      {selectedNode && (
        <>
          <h4>Selected Node: {selectedNode.name}</h4>
          <div>Value: {selectedNode.value}</div>
        </>
      )}
    </>
  );
};
```

#### Legend

The purpose of the legend is to provide additional identifying information for
the chart, without needing to interact with the legend directly.

##### Visibility

The legend of the `TreeMap` is shown by default. In order to hide or show the
legend, you need to set the value of `legend.hidden` on the subcomponent.

##### Position

By default, the position of the legend of the `TreeMap` is set automatically.
This option prioritizes the legend placement to the right of the chart area.
When the chart width is reduced, the legend is repositioned to the area beneath
the chart. It is also possible to explicitly set the chart's legend position to
right or bottom with the `position` prop.

##### Legend ratio

By default, the legend occupies `25%` of the container width, in the case where
the legend is positioned on the right and `25%` of the container height if the
legend position is on the bottom.

It is possible to override the default legend ratio by setting a custom
percentage value for the ratio prop. The expected value is in the range of
`5-80`. Values out of expected ranges will roll back to the default legend
ratio.

```tsx
      </TreeMap>
```

```tsx
const Legend = () => {
  return (
    <Flex gap={8} flexDirection="column">
      <TreeMap data={population}>
        <TreeMap.Legend hidden={true} />
      </TreeMap>
      <TreeMap data={population}>
        <TreeMap.Legend hidden={false} position="right" initialRatio={25} />
      </TreeMap>
      <TreeMap data={population}>
        <TreeMap.Legend hidden={false} position="bottom" initialRatio={25} />
      </TreeMap>
    </Flex>
  );
};
```


#### Formatter

The `TreeMap` component expects a structured tree object with a value and an
optional unit, by default this optional unit will be appended to the specified
value, if it is included. There are two other options in the formatter that
allow for greater customization. The first option enables you to prepend the
unit to the value, while the second option enables you to ignore the original
unit and append a custom string instead. Additionally, there is a custom
formatter option available to allow you to change the input unit to one of your
choice, e.g.: if the input unit is `bits`, you are able to switch and display
the unit as `bytes`, correctly formatted. The formatted value is applied in the
tooltip. The use cases below outline each of these scenarios.

```tsx
  return (
    <Flex gap={8} flexDirection="column">
      <TreeMap data={sales} formatter={undefined} />

      <TreeMap
        data={sales}
        formatter={(value, unit) => `${unit} ${Math.round(value)}`}
      />

      <TreeMap data={sales} formatter={{ suffix: 'money' }} />

      <TreeMap
        data={population}
        formatter={(value, unit) => `${unit} ${Math.round(value)}`}
      />

      <TreeMap
        data={population}
        formatter={{
          abbreviate: true,
          maximumFractionDigits: 10,
        }}
      />

      <TreeMap
        data={population}
        formatter={{
          // TODO: Adapt to non deprecated code - APPDEV-10743
          // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-deprecated
          input: units.currency.usd,
        }}
      />
    </Flex>
  );
```
```tsx
};

const Formatter = () => {
  return (
    <Flex gap={8} flexDirection="column">
      <TreeMap data={sales} formatter={undefined} />

      <TreeMap
        data={sales}
        formatter={(value, unit) => `${unit} ${Math.round(value)}`}
      />

      <TreeMap data={sales} formatter={{ suffix: 'money' }} />

      <TreeMap
        data={population}
        formatter={(value, unit) => `${unit} ${Math.round(value)}`}
      />

      <TreeMap
        data={population}
        formatter={{
          abbreviate: true,
          maximumFractionDigits: 10,
        }}
      />

      <TreeMap
        data={population}
        formatter={{
          // TODO: Adapt to non deprecated code - APPDEV-10743
          // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-deprecated
          input: units.currency.usd,
        }}
      />
    </Flex>
  );
};
```


#### Toolbar

The toolbar is where you can download data and use added Intents. It is
displayed by default, but to customize it, you can add the `TreeMap.Toolbar`
subcomponent, which supports the optional `hidden` and `placement` props.

```tsx
import { TreeMap, TreeMapData } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';

const data: TreeMapData = {
  tree: {
    name: 'Products',
    value: 49829490152,
    nodes: [
      {
        name: 'Electronics',
        value: 14514935852,
        nodes: [
          {
            name: 'Laptop',
            value: 753198634,
          },
          {
            name: 'Smartphone',
            value: 451994563,
          },
          {
            name: 'Tablet',
            value: 904609560,
          },
          {
            name: 'Headphone',
            value: 96251227,
          },
          {
            name: 'Camera',
            value: 915941,
          },
        ],
      },
      {
        name: 'Clothing',
        value: 2782578138,
        nodes: [
          {
            name: 'Shirt',
            value: 357341335,
          },
          {
            name: 'Jeans',
            value: 413329215,
          },
          {
            name: 'Jacket',
            value: 915314258,
          },
        ],
      },
      {
        name: 'Books',
        value: 2212496555,
        nodes: [
          {
            name: 'Fiction',
            value: 265831248,
          },
          {
            name: 'Non-fiction',
            value: 755363149,
          },
        ],
      },
      {
        name: 'Food',
        value: 2758749398,
        nodes: [
          {
            name: 'Pizza',
            value: 665491136,
          },
          {
            name: 'Burger',
            value: 237486948,
          },
          {
            name: 'Salad',
            value: 223351515,
          },
          {
            name: 'Sushi',
            value: 854585672,
          },
          {
            name: 'Cake',
            value: 343538825,
          },
          {
            name: 'Ice cream',
            value: 434295302,
          },
        ],
      },
    ],
  },
  unit: 'USD',
};

const Toolbar = () => {
  return (
    <TreeMap data={data} height={500}>
      <TreeMap.Toolbar placement="bottom-left" />
    </TreeMap>
  );
};
```

##### Behavioral tracking

The `TreeMap.Toolbar` subcomponent supports behavioral tracking attributes that
are spread onto the toolbar root element. This allows tracking of toolbar
interactions such as menu opens, mode changes, and download actions.

Pass any `data-dt-*` attributes directly on `TreeMap.Toolbar` to attach tracking
metadata. See `BehaviorTrackingProps` for the full list of supported attributes.

```tsx
const data: TreeMapData = {
  tree: {
    name: 'Products',
    value: 49829490152,
    nodes: [
      {
        name: 'Electronics',
        value: 14514935852,
        nodes: [
          { name: 'Laptop', value: 753198634 },
          { name: 'Smartphone', value: 451994563 },
          { name: 'Tablet', value: 904609560 },
        ],
      },
      {
        name: 'Clothing',
        value: 2782578138,
        nodes: [
          { name: 'Shirt', value: 357341335 },
          { name: 'Jeans', value: 413329215 },
        ],
      },
      {
        name: 'Books',
        value: 2212496555,
        nodes: [
          { name: 'Fiction', value: 265831248 },
          { name: 'Non-fiction', value: 755363149 },
        ],
      },
    ],
  },
  unit: 'USD',
};
```


#### Download data as CSV

The `TreeMap` component supports download data in CSV format using a toolbar
button. To enable this feature, a `TreeMap.DownloadCSV` subcomponent must be
provided to the `TreeMap` component. On click of the download button, raw data
will be downloaded as a CSV file.

The CSV file contains the following columns:

- `name` - the name of the node

- `value` - the value of the node

- `parent` - the parent of the node

It's also possible to programmatically trigger the download of the CSV file by
calling the `downloadData` method on the `TreeMap` instance reference.



#### Intent options

The `TreeMap` supports intent options in the toolbar. The intents appear in the
toolbar's dropdown menu, allowing users to perform actions such as sharing a
chart or viewing data in another application.

When a single intent is configured and no download button is shown, the intent
appears directly in the toolbar. When multiple intents are configured, or when
both an intent and a download button are present, they are grouped under a
More options submenu.

To add intent options to a `TreeMap`, use the `TreeMap.Intent` subcomponent:

##### Intent properties

- `payload`: An object containing the data to be passed to the target app. The
structure depends on the target application's requirements.

- `options`: Configuration options for the intent.

- `keyProperties`: Array of properties that should be included as keys in the
intent.

- `recommendedAppId`: Suggested target application ID.

- `recommendedIntentId`: Suggested intent ID.

- `responseProperties`: Array of properties to be included in the response.

- `icon`: Optional custom icon to be displayed next to the intent option.

- `onResponse`: Optional callback function that is called when a response is
received from the target app.

##### Examples

The following examples demonstrate different intent options in TreeMap:

```tsx

const sampleQuery =
  'data record(timestamp = now() - 1h, cpu_usage = random() * 100), record(timestamp = now() - 45m, cpu_usage = random() * 100), record(timestamp = now() - 30m, cpu_usage = random() * 100), record(timestamp = now() - 15m, cpu_usage = random() * 100), record(timestamp = now(), cpu_usage = random() * 100) | makeTimeseries interval:15m, cpu_usage = avg(cpu_usage)';

const IntentOptions = () => {
  return (
    <Flex gap={32} flexDirection="column">
      <Flex flexDirection="column" gap={8}>
        <Heading>Multiple Intent Options</Heading>
        <Text>
          You can add multiple intent options to a chart to provide users with
          different ways to interact with the data.
        </Text>
        <TreeMap data={sales}>
          <TreeMap.Intent
            payload={{
              'dt.query': 'fetch logs',
            }}
            options={{
              keyProperties: [],
            }}
            icon={<LineChartIcon />}
          >
            View logs
          </TreeMap.Intent>
          <TreeMap.Intent
            payload={{
              'dt.query': sampleQuery,
            }}
            options={{
              recommendedAppId: 'dynatrace.notebooks',
              recommendedIntentId: 'view-query',
            }}
            icon={<UfoIcon />}
          >
            Edit in notebook
          </TreeMap.Intent>
          <TreeMap.Intent
            payload={{
              'dt.query': sampleQuery,
            }}
            options={{
              recommendedAppId: 'dynatrace.dashboards',
              recommendedIntentId: 'add-query-to-dashboard',
            }}
          >
            Pin to dashboard
          </TreeMap.Intent>
          <TreeMap.Intent
            payload={{ 'dt.query': sampleQuery }}
            options={{
              keyProperties: [],
            }}
          >
            Open with ...
          </TreeMap.Intent>
        </TreeMap>
      </Flex>
    </Flex>
  );
};
```

```tsx

const sampleQuery =
  'data record(timestamp = now() - 1h, cpu_usage = random() * 100), record(timestamp = now() - 45m, cpu_usage = random() * 100), record(timestamp = now() - 30m, cpu_usage = random() * 100), record(timestamp = now() - 15m, cpu_usage = random() * 100), record(timestamp = now(), cpu_usage = random() * 100) | makeTimeseries interval:15m, cpu_usage = avg(cpu_usage)';

const IntentOptions = () => {
  return (
    <Flex gap={32} flexDirection="column">
      <Flex flexDirection="column" gap={8}>
        <Heading>Multiple Intent Options</Heading>
        <Text>
          You can add multiple intent options to a chart to provide users with
          different ways to interact with the data.
        </Text>
        <TreeMap data={sales}>
          <TreeMap.Intent
            payload={{
              'dt.query': 'fetch logs',
            }}
            options={{
              keyProperties: [],
            }}
            icon={<LineChartIcon />}
          >
            View logs
          </TreeMap.Intent>
          <TreeMap.Intent
            payload={{
              'dt.query': sampleQuery,
            }}
            options={{
              recommendedAppId: 'dynatrace.notebooks',
              recommendedIntentId: 'view-query',
            }}
            icon={<UfoIcon />}
          >
            Edit in notebook
          </TreeMap.Intent>
          <TreeMap.Intent
            payload={{
              'dt.query': sampleQuery,
            }}
            options={{
              recommendedAppId: 'dynatrace.dashboards',
              recommendedIntentId: 'add-query-to-dashboard',
            }}
          >
            Pin to dashboard
          </TreeMap.Intent>
          <TreeMap.Intent
            payload={{ 'dt.query': sampleQuery }}
            options={{
              keyProperties: [],
            }}
          >
            Open with ...
          </TreeMap.Intent>
        </TreeMap>
      </Flex>
    </Flex>
  );
};
```


#### Label Display

The `TreeMap` component offers a `labelsDisplay` prop to control how labels are
presented.

When `labelsDisplay` is set to `cluster`, labels will appear above each group of
nodes, indicating the name of the parent cluster. When `labelDisplay` is set to
`nodes`, labels will be displayed within each node, showing the name of the
individual child node.

These options can be combined by setting the `labelDisplay` prop to `all`. In
this mode, both parent cluster names and individual child node names will be
displayed simultaneously.

```tsx
import { TreeMap, type TreeMapData } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';
import { Flex } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/layouts';

const population: TreeMapData = {
  tree: {

      <Flex flexDirection="column">
        <h2>Cluster and Node Labels Combined</h2>
        <TreeMap data={population} labelsDisplay="all" />
      </Flex>
    </Flex>
  );
};
```

#### Coloring

The `TreeMap` component provides a set of predefined color palettes that can be
used out of the box to provide more appealing visualisations. See
Charts Colors for more info.

```tsx
import {
  TreeMap,
  type ColoredRange,
  type TreeMapData,
} from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';
import { Flex } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/layouts';
import Colors from '@dynatrace/strato-design-tokens/colors';

const population: TreeMapData = {
  tree: {
    name: '',
    value: 0,
    nodes: [
      {
        name: 'India',
        value: 117200000,
        nodes: [
          {
            name: 'Delhi',
            value: 27000000,
          },
          {
            name: 'Mumbai',
            value: 21600000,
          },

            name: 'Kolkata',
            value: 15600000,
          },

            name: 'Chennai',
            value: 11100000,
          },
          {
            name: 'Bangalore',
            value: 10800000,
          },
          {
            name: 'Hyderabad',
            value: 9900000,
          },
          {
            name: 'Ahmedabad',
            value: 8100000,
          },
          {
            name: 'Pune',
            value: 6600000,
          },
          {
            name: 'Surat',
            value: 6500000,
          },
        ],
      },
      {
        name: 'China',
        value: 166200000,
        nodes: [
          {
            name: 'Shanghai',
            value: 25600000,
          },
          {
            name: 'Beijing',
            value: 22000000,
          },
          {
            name: 'Chongqing',
            value: 16300000,
          },
          {
            name: 'Tianjin',
            value: 13600000,
          },
          {
            name: 'Guangzhou',
            value: 13100000,
          },
          {
            name: 'Shenzhen',
            value: 12400000,
          },
          {
            name: 'Chengdu',
            value: 10200000,
          },
          {
            name: 'Nanjing',
            value: 9300000,
          },
          {
            name: 'Wuhan',
            value: 8500000,
          },
          {
            name: 'Hangzhou',
            value: 7800000,
          },
          {
            name: 'Xi\u2019an',
            value: 7100000,
          },
          {
            name: 'Changsha',
            value: 6900000,
          },
          {
            name: 'Harbin',
            value: 6800000,
          },
          {

const Coloring = () => {
  return (
    <Flex gap={12} flexDirection="column">
      <Flex flexDirection="column">
        <h2>Coloring by Cluster</h2>
        <TreeMap
          data={population}
          colorPalette="blue-steel"
          labelsDisplay="clusters"
        />
      </Flex>

      <Flex flexDirection="column">
        <h2>Coloring by nameAccessor + log-level </h2>
        <TreeMap data={servers} colorPalette="log-level" nameAccessor="name" />
      </Flex>

      <Flex flexDirection="column">
        <h2>Coloring by nameAccessor + random palette (unmatched nodes)</h2>
        <TreeMap data={servers} colorPalette="blue-steel" nameAccessor="name" />
      </Flex>

      <Flex flexDirection="column">
        <h2>Coloring by node names</h2>
        <TreeMap
          data={population}
          colorPalette="categorical"
          labelsDisplay="clusters"
          nameAccessor="name"
        />
      </Flex>

      <Flex flexDirection="column">
        <h2>Coloring by node values</h2>
        <TreeMap
          data={population}
          colorPalette="turquoise"
          labelsDisplay="clusters"
          nameAccessor="value"
        />
      </Flex>

      <Flex flexDirection="column">
        <h2>Coloring by custom node value ranges</h2>
        <TreeMap
          data={userSessions}
          colorPalette={customRanges}
          labelsDisplay="clusters"
          nameAccessor="value"
        />
      </Flex>
    </Flex>
  );
};
```

```tsx
  tree: {
    name: '',
    value: 0,

      {
        name: 'Europe',
        value: 420,

          {
            name: 'Rome',

const Coloring = () => {
  return (
    <Flex gap={12} flexDirection="column">
      <Flex flexDirection="column">
        <h2>Coloring by Cluster</h2>
        <TreeMap
          data={population}
          colorPalette="blue-steel"
          labelsDisplay="clusters"
        />
      </Flex>

      <Flex flexDirection="column">
        <h2>Coloring by nameAccessor + log-level </h2>
        <TreeMap data={servers} colorPalette="log-level" nameAccessor="name" />
      </Flex>

      <Flex flexDirection="column">
        <h2>Coloring by nameAccessor + random palette (unmatched nodes)</h2>
        <TreeMap data={servers} colorPalette="blue-steel" nameAccessor="name" />
      </Flex>

      <Flex flexDirection="column">
        <h2>Coloring by node names</h2>
        <TreeMap
          data={population}
          colorPalette="categorical"
          labelsDisplay="clusters"
          nameAccessor="name"
        />
      </Flex>

      <Flex flexDirection="column">
        <h2>Coloring by node values</h2>
        <TreeMap
          data={population}
          colorPalette="turquoise"
          labelsDisplay="clusters"
          nameAccessor="value"
        />
      </Flex>

      <Flex flexDirection="column">
        <h2>Coloring by custom node value ranges</h2>
        <TreeMap
          data={userSessions}
          colorPalette={customRanges}
          labelsDisplay="clusters"
          nameAccessor="value"
        />
      </Flex>
    </Flex>
  );
};
```


##### Color Rules

The `TreeMap` component supports conditional coloring based on custom rules
using the `TreeMap.ColorRule` slot. Color rules allow you to apply specific
colors to nodes based on their properties, such as value comparisons or string
matching.

Each `TreeMap.ColorRule` accepts the following properties:

- `comparator`: The comparison operator (e.g., 'greater-than', 'equals',
'starts-with', 'contains').

- `matchValue`: The value to compare against.

- `valueAccessor`: Optional, specifies which property of the node to evaluate.
When omitted, the default target depends on the chart's `nameAccessor` prop:
if `nameAccessor="value"` the rule evaluates `node.value`; otherwise it
evaluates `node.name`. For explicit numerical comparisons on node values,
always set `valueAccessor="value"`.

- `color` and `colorPalette` are mutually exclusive.

- `color`: A specific color string (e.g., 'red', '#ff0000').

- `colorPalette`: A predefined color palette name.

Rules are evaluated in the order they are defined, and the last matching rule
applies.

Slot Usage Examples:

`tsx
TreeMap data={data} colorPalette="fireplace"> {/* Numerical comparison with single color */} TreeMap.ColorRule comparator="greater-than" matchValue={100} color="red" /> {/* String matching with color palette */} TreeMap.ColorRule comparator="starts-with" matchValue="Error" colorPalette="log-level" /> {/* Custom property access */} TreeMap.ColorRule comparator="greater-than" matchValue={50} color="blue" valueAccessor="temperature" />TreeMap>
`

```tsx
import { TreeMap, type TreeMapData } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';
import { Flex } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/layouts';

// Sample data for testing color rules
const sampleTreeMapData: TreeMapData = {
  tree: {
    name: 'Root',
    value: 200,
    nodes: [
      {
        name: 'Category A',
        value: 40,
        nodes: [
          { name: 'Item A1', value: 20 },
          { name: 'Item A2', value: 8, temperature: 15 },
        ],
      },
      {
        name: 'Category B',
        value: 100,
        nodes: [
          { name: 'Item B1', value: 40 },
          { name: 'Item B2', value: 30 },
          { name: 'Item B3', value: 30 },
        ],
      },
      {
        name: 'Category C',
        value: 60,
        nodes: [
          { name: 'Item C1', value: 10 },
          { name: 'Item C2', value: 10 },
          { name: 'Item C3', value: 10 },
          { name: 'Item C4', value: 10 },
          { name: 'Item C5', value: 10 },
          { name: 'Item C6', value: 10 },
        ],
      },
    ],
  },
  unit: 'count',
};

// Data with different comparators
const comparatorData: TreeMapData = {
  tree: {
    name: 'Servers',
    value: 100,
    nodes: [
      {
        name: 'cluster',
        value: 100,
        nodes: [
          { name: 'Error Server', value: 25, status: 'error' },
          { name: 'Warning Server', value: 30, status: 'warning' },
          { name: 'Normal Server', value: 35, status: 'normal' },
          { name: 'Critical Server', value: 10, status: 'critical' },
        ],
      },
    ],
  },
  unit: 'cpu',
};

// Data with custom properties
const customPropertyData: TreeMapData = {
  tree: {
    name: 'Devices',
    value: 100,
    nodes: [
      {
        name: 'cluster',
        value: 100,
        nodes: [
          { name: 'Device 1', value: 20, battery: 95, location: 'US' },
          { name: 'Device 2', value: 25, battery: 75, location: 'EU' },
          { name: 'Device 3', value: 30, battery: 45, location: 'US' },
          { name: 'Device 4', value: 25, battery: 20, location: 'ASIA' },
        ],
      },
    ],
  },
  unit: 'usage',
};

const ColorRules = () => {
  return (
    <Flex gap={12} flexDirection="column">
      <Flex flexDirection="column">
        <h2>Numerical Color Rules</h2>
        <TreeMap
          data={sampleTreeMapData}
          labelsDisplay="all"
          nameAccessor="value"
          colorPalette="fireplace"
        >
          <TreeMap.ColorRule
            comparator="greater-than"
            matchValue={19}
            color="#a1e076"
          />
          <TreeMap.ColorRule
            comparator="greater-or-equal"
            matchValue={40}
            color="rebeccapurple"
          />
          <TreeMap.ColorRule
            comparator="greater-than"
            matchValue={10}
            color="blue"
            valueAccessor="temperature"
          />
        </TreeMap>
      </Flex>

      <Flex flexDirection="column">
        <h2>Categorical Color Rules</h2>
        <TreeMap
          data={sampleTreeMapData}
          nameAccessor="name"
          colorPalette="swamps"
          labelsDisplay="all"
        >
          <TreeMap.ColorRule
            comparator="starts-with"
            matchValue="Item B"
            color="red"
          />
          <TreeMap.ColorRule
            comparator="contains"
            matchValue="A"
            color="rebeccapurple"
          />
          <TreeMap.ColorRule
            comparator="ends-with"
            matchValue="1"
            color="blue"
          />
        </TreeMap>
      </Flex>

      <Flex flexDirection="column">
        <h2>Different Comparators</h2>
        <TreeMap
          data={comparatorData}
          nameAccessor="name"
          colorPalette="categorical"
          labelsDisplay="all"
        >
          <TreeMap.ColorRule
            comparator="equals"
            matchValue="error"
            color="red"
            valueAccessor="status"
          />
          <TreeMap.ColorRule
            comparator="starts-with"
            matchValue="Warning"
            color="orange"
            valueAccessor="status"
          />
          <TreeMap.ColorRule
            comparator="greater-than"
            matchValue={20}
            color="green"
          />
          <TreeMap.ColorRule
            comparator="less-or-equal"
            matchValue={15}
            color="purple"
          />
        </TreeMap>
      </Flex>

      <Flex flexDirection="column">
        <h2>Custom Properties (valueAccessor)</h2>
        <TreeMap
          data={customPropertyData}
          nameAccessor="name"
          colorPalette="turquoise"
          labelsDisplay="all"
        >
          <TreeMap.ColorRule
            comparator="greater-than"
            matchValue={80}
            color="green"
            valueAccessor="battery"
          />
          <TreeMap.ColorRule
            comparator="less-or-equal"
            matchValue={50}
            color="red"
            valueAccessor="battery"
          />
          <TreeMap.ColorRule
            comparator="equals"
            matchValue="US"
            color="blue"
            valueAccessor="location"
          />
        </TreeMap>
      </Flex>

      <Flex flexDirection="column">
        <h2>Rule Precedence (Order Matters)</h2>
        <TreeMap
          data={sampleTreeMapData}
          nameAccessor="value"
          colorPalette="fireplace"
          labelsDisplay="all"
        >
          {/* This rule would color values >= 10, but the next rule takes precedence for >= 40 */}
          <TreeMap.ColorRule
            comparator="greater-or-equal"
            matchValue={10}
            color="yellow"
          />
          <TreeMap.ColorRule
            comparator="greater-or-equal"
            matchValue={40}
            color="red"
          />
          <TreeMap.ColorRule
            comparator="greater-than"
            matchValue={19}
            color="green"
          />
        </TreeMap>
      </Flex>

      <Flex flexDirection="column">
        <h2>Single Colors vs Color Palettes</h2>
        <TreeMap
          data={sampleTreeMapData}
          nameAccessor="name"
          colorPalette="categorical"
          labelsDisplay="all"
        >
          {/* Single color */}
          <TreeMap.ColorRule
            comparator="starts-with"
            matchValue="Item A"
            color="#ff6b6b"
          />
          {/* Color palette */}
          <TreeMap.ColorRule
            comparator="starts-with"
            matchValue="Item B"
            colorPalette="rainbow"
          />
          {/* Another single color */}
          <TreeMap.ColorRule
            comparator="starts-with"
            matchValue="Item C"
            color="steelblue"
          />
        </TreeMap>
      </Flex>
    </Flex>
  );
};
```

```tsx
import { TreeMap, type TreeMapData } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';
import { Flex } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/layouts';

// Sample data for testing color rules
const sampleTreeMapData: TreeMapData = {
  tree: {
    name: 'Root',
    value: 200,
    nodes: [
      {
        name: 'Category A',
        value: 40,
        nodes: [
          { name: 'Item A1', value: 20 },
          { name: 'Item A2', value: 8, temperature: 15 },
        ],
      },
      {
        name: 'Category B',
        value: 100,
        nodes: [
          { name: 'Item B1', value: 40 },
          { name: 'Item B2', value: 30 },
          { name: 'Item B3', value: 30 },
        ],
      },
      {
        name: 'Category C',
        value: 60,
        nodes: [
          { name: 'Item C1', value: 10 },
          { name: 'Item C2', value: 10 },
          { name: 'Item C3', value: 10 },
          { name: 'Item C4', value: 10 },
          { name: 'Item C5', value: 10 },
          { name: 'Item C6', value: 10 },
        ],
      },
    ],
  },
  unit: 'count',
};

// Data with different comparators
const comparatorData: TreeMapData = {
  tree: {
    name: 'Servers',
    value: 100,
    nodes: [
      {
        name: 'cluster',
        value: 100,
        nodes: [
          { name: 'Error Server', value: 25, status: 'error' },
          { name: 'Warning Server', value: 30, status: 'warning' },
          { name: 'Normal Server', value: 35, status: 'normal' },
          { name: 'Critical Server', value: 10, status: 'critical' },
        ],
      },
    ],
  },
  unit: 'cpu',
};

// Data with custom properties
const customPropertyData: TreeMapData = {
  tree: {
    name: 'Devices',
    value: 100,
    nodes: [
      {
        name: 'cluster',
        value: 100,
        nodes: [
          { name: 'Device 1', value: 20, battery: 95, location: 'US' },
          { name: 'Device 2', value: 25, battery: 75, location: 'EU' },
          { name: 'Device 3', value: 30, battery: 45, location: 'US' },
          { name: 'Device 4', value: 25, battery: 20, location: 'ASIA' },
        ],
      },
    ],
  },
  unit: 'usage',
};

const ColorRules = () => {
  return (
    <Flex gap={12} flexDirection="column">
      <Flex flexDirection="column">
        <h2>Numerical Color Rules</h2>
        <TreeMap
          data={sampleTreeMapData}
          labelsDisplay="all"
          nameAccessor="value"
          colorPalette="fireplace"
        >
          <TreeMap.ColorRule
            comparator="greater-than"
            matchValue={19}
            color="#a1e076"
          />
          <TreeMap.ColorRule
            comparator="greater-or-equal"
            matchValue={40}
            color="rebeccapurple"
          />
          <TreeMap.ColorRule
            comparator="greater-than"
            matchValue={10}
            color="blue"
            valueAccessor="temperature"
          />
        </TreeMap>
      </Flex>

      <Flex flexDirection="column">
        <h2>Categorical Color Rules</h2>
        <TreeMap
          data={sampleTreeMapData}
          nameAccessor="name"
          colorPalette="swamps"
          labelsDisplay="all"
        >
          <TreeMap.ColorRule
            comparator="starts-with"
            matchValue="Item B"
            color="red"
          />
          <TreeMap.ColorRule
            comparator="contains"
            matchValue="A"
            color="rebeccapurple"
          />
          <TreeMap.ColorRule
            comparator="ends-with"
            matchValue="1"
            color="blue"
          />
        </TreeMap>
      </Flex>

      <Flex flexDirection="column">
        <h2>Different Comparators</h2>
        <TreeMap
          data={comparatorData}
          nameAccessor="name"
          colorPalette="categorical"
          labelsDisplay="all"
        >
          <TreeMap.ColorRule
            comparator="equals"
            matchValue="error"
            color="red"
            valueAccessor="status"
          />
          <TreeMap.ColorRule
            comparator="starts-with"
            matchValue="Warning"
            color="orange"
            valueAccessor="status"
          />
          <TreeMap.ColorRule
            comparator="greater-than"
            matchValue={20}
            color="green"
          />
          <TreeMap.ColorRule
            comparator="less-or-equal"
            matchValue={15}
            color="purple"
          />
        </TreeMap>
      </Flex>

      <Flex flexDirection="column">
        <h2>Custom Properties (valueAccessor)</h2>
        <TreeMap
          data={customPropertyData}
          nameAccessor="name"
          colorPalette="turquoise"
          labelsDisplay="all"
        >
          <TreeMap.ColorRule
            comparator="greater-than"
            matchValue={80}
            color="green"
            valueAccessor="battery"
          />
          <TreeMap.ColorRule
            comparator="less-or-equal"
            matchValue={50}
            color="red"
            valueAccessor="battery"
          />
          <TreeMap.ColorRule
            comparator="equals"
            matchValue="US"
            color="blue"
            valueAccessor="location"
          />
        </TreeMap>
      </Flex>

      <Flex flexDirection="column">
        <h2>Rule Precedence (Order Matters)</h2>
        <TreeMap
          data={sampleTreeMapData}
          nameAccessor="value"
          colorPalette="fireplace"
          labelsDisplay="all"
        >
          {/* This rule would color values >= 10, but the next rule takes precedence for >= 40 */}
          <TreeMap.ColorRule
            comparator="greater-or-equal"
            matchValue={10}
            color="yellow"
          />
          <TreeMap.ColorRule
            comparator="greater-or-equal"
            matchValue={40}
            color="red"
          />
          <TreeMap.ColorRule
            comparator="greater-than"
            matchValue={19}
            color="green"
          />
        </TreeMap>
      </Flex>

      <Flex flexDirection="column">
        <h2>Single Colors vs Color Palettes</h2>
        <TreeMap
          data={sampleTreeMapData}
          nameAccessor="name"
          colorPalette="categorical"
          labelsDisplay="all"
        >
          {/* Single color */}
          <TreeMap.ColorRule
            comparator="starts-with"
            matchValue="Item A"
            color="#ff6b6b"
          />
          {/* Color palette */}
          <TreeMap.ColorRule
            comparator="starts-with"
            matchValue="Item B"
            colorPalette="rainbow"
          />
          {/* Another single color */}
          <TreeMap.ColorRule
            comparator="starts-with"
            matchValue="Item C"
            color="steelblue"
          />
        </TreeMap>
      </Flex>
    </Flex>
  );
};
```


#### Error state

The `ErrorState` subcomponent is responsible for handling errors in a graceful
manner, ultimately improving the overall user experience. Its primary function
is to catch any errors that may occur with the data and display a fallback UI
instead of crashing the entire application. The fallback UI occupies the full
width and height of the chart, ensuring that users are still provided with a
meaningful interface even in the presence of errors.

The `ErrorState` subcomponent offers a versatile feature that enables it to
handle both default and custom error messages. You can provide a custom message
through the `ErrorState` subcomponent, which will then override the default
error message. This flexibility allows developers to tailor error messages to
their specific needs and requirements, ensuring a more personalized and
informative user experience.

The `ErrorState` subcomponent provides the flexibility to format custom error
messages using HTML, which allows for enhanced customization and adaptability in
presenting error information. Furthermore, it is possible to incorporate the
original thrown error within your custom error message, ensuring that users
receive comprehensive and relevant information when an error occurs.

#### EmptyState

The `EmptyState` subcomponent serves as a fallback when there is no data
available to display in a chart. Its purpose is to provide a user-friendly way
of informing the user about the current situation. When there is no data, a
fallback UI is displayed occupying the full width and height of the chart, along
with a default message.

A feature of `EmptyState` is its ability to handle custom messages. It provides
the flexibility to format custom messages using HTML, which allows for enhanced
customization and adaptability in presenting error information.

```tsx
import { TreeMap } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';

const EmptyStateDefault = () => {
  return <TreeMap data={{ tree: { nodes: [], name: '', value: 0 } }} />;
};
```

```tsx
import { TreeMap } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';

const EmptyStateDefault = () => {
  return <TreeMap data={{ tree: { nodes: [], name: '', value: 0 } }} />;
};
```


#### Loading

The `loading` prop is a boolean value that can be passed to the `TreeMap`
component to control its loading state. When the loading prop is set to true,
the loading indicator appears in the middle of the chart plot to inform the user
that the component is currently fetching or processing data. When the loading
prop is set to false, the component should display its regular content.

```tsx
import { TreeMap } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';

const LoadingWithOutData = () => {
  return (
    <TreeMap
      data={{ tree: { nodes: [], name: '', value: 0 } }}
      loading={true}
    />
  );
};
```

```tsx
import { TreeMap } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';

const LoadingWithOutData = () => {
  return (
    <TreeMap
      data={{ tree: { nodes: [], name: '', value: 0 } }}
      loading={true}
    />
  );
};
```


#### Styling

The `TreeMap` also accepts custom styling, which could be set using the props
`className` and/or `style`, as a regular html element

### Props

The `TreeMap` visualizes the distribution of hierarchical data. It uses nested
rectangles of different sizes and colors to show how much each category or
subcategory contributes to the whole data set.

OverviewProperties

#### TreeMapProps
extends`, , , ` |
 | Name | Type | Default | Description
 | `data` | | | The tree map data to display.
 | `width?` | | | `100%` | Optional width of the chart.
 | `height?` | | | `400` | Optional height of the chart.
 | `colorPalette?` | | | [] | `'categorical'` | Color palette to use for coloring the tree nodes. Nodes of height 1
get a color assigned from the palette.
 | `formatter?` | | | | Custom value formatter for the chart.
 | `unit?` | | | | Optional unit for value formatting. Takes priority over data.unit
 | `truncationMode?` | | `'middle'` | The text truncation mode used for the chart legend.
 | `loading?` | | `false` | Show the loading indicator.
 | `labelsDisplay?` | | `'none'` | Show/hide label on TreeMap nodes and clusters
 | `seriesActions?` | (node: ) => | | Exposed callback to display series actions for a node
 | `nameAccessor?` | | `undefined` | Data accessor on leaf nodes by which legend can be defined
 | `min?` | | | | The min value configuration to display
 | `max?` | | | | The max value configuration to display

### TreeMap.Legend

To configure the legend, add `TreeMap.Legend` to the TreeMap chart.

#### TreemapChartLegendConfig

##### Signature:
`export declare type TreemapChartLegendConfig = ;`

### TreeMap.Tooltip

To configure the tooltip, add `TreeMap.Tooltip` to the TreeMap chart.

### TreeMap.EmptyState

`TreeMap.EmptyState` provides a slot where the Empty state wrapper can be set.

#### EmptyStateProp
 |
 | Name | Type | Default | Description
 | `children` | | |

### TreeMap.ErrorStateSlotProps

`TreeMap.ErrorState` provides a slot where the Error state wrapper can be set.

#### ErrorStateProps
 |
 | Name | Type | Default | Description
 | `children` | | ((errorMessage: ) => .) | |

### TreeMap.Toolbar

`TreeMap.Toolbar` is a slot to hide or to configure the toolbar.

#### CompactToolbarProps

##### Signature:
`export declare type CompactToolbarProps = & {
 hidden?: ;
 placement?: [];
};`Still have questions?Find answers in the Dynatrace Community

## XYChart

`/design/data-visualizations/charts/XYChart/`

The `XYChart` encompasses various types of charts that utilize both x-axis and
y-axis for data representation, designed to visually display and analyze data
that involve two numerical variables.

OverviewProperties

### Import

`tsx
import { XYChart } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';
`

### Use cases

The `XYChart` encompasses various types of charts that utilize both X-axis and
Y-axis for data representation, designed to visually display and analyze data
that involve two numerical variables. The following variants are currently
supported: RectSeries,
DotSeries,
LineSeries and
BarSeries.

#### Data

The data provided to the `XYChart` component via the `data` property will be
used in combination with the accessors of each series.

The series subcomponent has an optional `data` property that can be used to
provide specific data for that series.

Learn more about the data format here.

#### Data accessors

Accessors specify how to retrieve data from your data structure. Accessor
strings that contain dots allow you to retrieve nested data. If the actual
property key contains a dot, you can escape an accessor by enclosing the string
in square brackets.

```tsx
import { XYChart } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';

const data = [
  {
    horizontal: {
      'number(of.host.available)': {
        min: 0,
      },
      'number(of.host.[available]).max': { value: 50000 },
    },
    vertical: {
      cpu: {
        usage: {
          level: {
            min: 0,
            max: 100,
          },
        },
      },
    },
    'host.primary': { 'host.secondary': 'CPU avg' },
  },
];

const Accessors = () => {
  return (
    <XYChart data={data}>
      <XYChart.RectSeries
        xAxisId="x-first-axis"
        yAxisId="y-first-axis"
        x0Accessor='horizontal["number(of.host.available)"].min'
        x1Accessor='horizontal["number(of.host.[available]).max"].value'
        y0Accessor="vertical.cpu.usage.level.min"
        y1Accessor='vertical.cpu.usage.["level"].max'
        valueAccessor='["host.primary"].["host.secondary"]'
      />
      <XYChart.XAxis
        id="x-first-axis"
        type="numerical"
        position="bottom"
        label="Number of hosts"
      />
      <XYChart.YAxis
        id="y-first-axis"
        type="numerical"
        position="left"
        unit="percent"
        label="CPU usage (%)"
      />
    </XYChart>
  );
};
```

```tsx
import { XYChart } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';

const data = [
  {
    horizontal: {
      'number(of.host.available)': {
        min: 0,
      },
      'number(of.host.[available]).max': { value: 50000 },
    },
    vertical: {
      cpu: {
        usage: {
          level: {
            min: 0,
            max: 100,
          },
        },
      },
    },
    'host.primary': { 'host.secondary': 'CPU avg' },
  },
];

const Accessors = () => {
  return (
    <XYChart data={data}>
      <XYChart.RectSeries
        xAxisId="x-first-axis"
        yAxisId="y-first-axis"
        x0Accessor='horizontal["number(of.host.available)"].min'
        x1Accessor='horizontal["number(of.host.[available]).max"].value'
        y0Accessor="vertical.cpu.usage.level.min"
        y1Accessor='vertical.cpu.usage.["level"].max'
        valueAccessor='["host.primary"].["host.secondary"]'
      />
      <XYChart.XAxis
        id="x-first-axis"
        type="numerical"
        position="bottom"
        label="Number of hosts"
      />
      <XYChart.YAxis
        id="y-first-axis"
        type="numerical"
        position="left"
        unit="percent"
        label="CPU usage (%)"
      />
    </XYChart>
  );
};
```


#### Truncation mode

The purpose of truncation is to gracefully handle extra long labels within data
visualization components. By changing the value of the `truncationMode`
property, you have control over where truncation is applied within charts. By
default, the truncation is applied to the middle of labels with the use of an
ellipsis. Truncation can, however, be changed to instead be applied at the start
or end of data visualization component labels.

#### Coloring

The `XYChart` provides several ways to customize the appearance of your data:

- Predefined Color Palettes: Use built-in color schemes

- Custom Color Palettes: Define your own color schemes

- Color Rules: Apply conditional coloring based on data values.

##### Predefined Color Palettes

The `XYChart`'s `colorPalette` property supports predefined color palettes,
which are useful for consistent theming across your application. See
coloring for more details.

```tsx
import { XYChart } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';

const categoryTimeDataset = [
  {
    Person: { start: 'Person2' },
    Year: { start: '2017-01-01', end: '2018-01-01' },
    EventsAssisted: 10,
  },
  {
    Person: { start: 'Person2' },
    Year: { start: '2018-01-01', end: '2019-01-01' },
    EventsAssisted: 30,
  },
  {
    Person: { start: 'Person2' },
    Year: { start: '2019-01-01', end: '2020-01-01' },
    EventsAssisted: 50,
  },
  {
    Person: { start: 'Person2' },
    Year: { start: '2020-01-01', end: '2021-01-01' },
    EventsAssisted: 100,
  },
  {
    Person: { start: 'Person1' },
    Year: { start: '2017-01-01', end: '2018-01-01' },
    EventsAssisted: 20,
  },
  {
    Person: { start: 'Person1' },
    Year: { start: '2018-01-01', end: '2019-01-01' },
    EventsAssisted: 50,
  },
  {
    Person: { start: 'Person1' },
    Year: { start: '2019-01-01', end: '2020-01-01' },
    EventsAssisted: 40,
  },
  {
    Person: { start: 'Person1' },
    Year: { start: '2020-01-01', end: '2021-01-01' },
    EventsAssisted: 20,
  },
];

const PredefinedColorPalette = () => {
  return (
    <XYChart data={categoryTimeDataset} colorPalette="pink">
      <XYChart.RectSeries
        xAxisId="x-first-axis"
        yAxisId="y-first-axis"
        x0Accessor="Person.start"
        y0Accessor="Year.start"
        y1Accessor="Year.end"
        valueAccessor="EventsAssisted"
      />
      <XYChart.XAxis id="x-first-axis" type="categorical" position="bottom" />
      <XYChart.YAxis id="y-first-axis" type="time" position="left" />
    </XYChart>
  );
};
```

```tsx
import { XYChart } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';

const categoryTimeDataset = [
  {
    Person: { start: 'Person2' },

  {

    EventsAssisted: 30,

  {

    Year: { start: '2019-01-01', end: '2020-01-01' },
    EventsAssisted: 50,
  },
  {
    Person: { start: 'Person2' },
    Year: { start: '2020-01-01', end: '2021-01-01' },
    EventsAssisted: 100,
  },
  {
    Person: { start: 'Person1' },
    Year: { start: '2017-01-01', end: '2018-01-01' },
    EventsAssisted: 20,
  },
  {
    Person: { start: 'Person1' },
    Year: { start: '2018-01-01', end: '2019-01-01' },
    EventsAssisted: 50,
  },
  {
    Person: { start: 'Person1' },
    Year: { start: '2019-01-01', end: '2020-01-01' },
    EventsAssisted: 40,
  },
  {
    Person: { start: 'Person1' },
    Year: { start: '2020-01-01', end: '2021-01-01' },
    EventsAssisted: 20,

];

const PredefinedColorPalette = () => {
  return (
    <XYChart data={categoryTimeDataset} colorPalette="pink">
      <XYChart.RectSeries
        xAxisId="x-first-axis"
        yAxisId="y-first-axis"
        x0Accessor="Person.start"
        y0Accessor="Year.start"
        y1Accessor="Year.end"
        valueAccessor="EventsAssisted"
      />
      <XYChart.XAxis id="x-first-axis" type="categorical" position="bottom" />
      <XYChart.YAxis id="y-first-axis" type="time" position="left" />
    </XYChart>
  );
};
```


##### Custom Color Palettes

Custom color palettes can be useful when you need specific colors for your data,
especially for categorical data of type string.

```tsx
  return (
    <XYChart
      data={randomNumber}
      colorPalette={{
        Error: '#be5d46',
        Info: '#6092c5',
        Warning: '#cdde42',
      }}
    >
      <XYChart.DotSeries
        xAxisId="x-first-axis"
        yAxisId="y-first-axis"
        x0Accessor="x0"
        y0Accessor="y0"
        nameAccessor="seriesName"
      />
      <XYChart.DotSeries
        xAxisId="x-first-axis"
        yAxisId="y-first-axis"
        x0Accessor="x1"
        y0Accessor="y1"
        nameAccessor="seriesName"
      />
      <XYChart.DotSeries
        xAxisId="x-first-axis"
        yAxisId="y-first-axis"
        x0Accessor="x2"
        y0Accessor="y2"
        nameAccessor="seriesName"
      />
      <XYChart.XAxis id="x-first-axis" type="numerical" position="bottom" />
      <XYChart.YAxis id="y-first-axis" type="numerical" position="left" />
    </XYChart>
  );
};
```

```tsx
      <XYChart.YAxis id="y-first-axis" type="numerical" position="left" />
    </XYChart>
  );
};
```

##### Using Color Rules

For advanced coloring scenarios, you can use the `XYChart.ColorRule` slot to
apply conditional coloring based on data values. Color Rule slots behave
differently depending on the series type:

- RectSeries (heatmaps): Applies solid colors to individual tiles that match
the rule

- DotSeries, LineSeries, BarSeries: Applies colors at the series level when
any datapoint matches

This is particularly useful for highlighting specific data points or ranges that
meet certain criteria, such as error states, thresholds, or specific categories.

###### Supported comparators

Color rules support various comparison operators that can be used to match data
values:

- Numerical comparisons: `'less-than'`, `'less-or-equal'`, `'greater-than'`,
`'greater-or-equal'`

- Equality comparisons: `'equals'`, `'not-equals'`

- String comparisons: `'starts-with'`, `'not-starts-with'`, `'ends-with'`,
`'not-ends-with'`, `'contains'`, `'not-contains'`, `'matches-phrase'`,
`'not-matches-phrase'`

You can apply rules to different data properties using the `valueAccessor` prop,
which defaults to the y-value if not specified. When multiple rules match a data
point, the last rule in the array takes precedence.

For more details about available comparators and options, see the
Color Rules
section.

##### Custom Color Ranges

Custom color ranges can be useful to apply gradient-based coloring to data of
type number.

```tsx
import { XYChart } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';

const numericalDataset = [
  {
    amountRange: {
      start: 0,
      end: -10,
    },
    peopleRange: {
      start: 0,
      end: 10,
    },
    events: 10,
  },

  {
    amountRange: {
      start: 0,
      end: -10,
    },
    peopleRange: {
      start: 10,
      end: 20,
    },
    events: 30,
  },
  {
    amountRange: {
      start: 0,
      end: -10,
    },
    peopleRange: {
      start: 20,
      end: 30,
    },
    events: 50,
  },
  {
    amountRange: {
      start: 0,
      end: -10,
    },
    peopleRange: {
      start: 30,
      end: 40,
    },
    events: 100,
  },
  {
    amountRange: {
      start: -10,
      end: -20,
    },
    peopleRange: {
      start: 0,
      end: 10,
    },
    events: 20,
  },
  {
    amountRange: {
      start: -10,
      end: -20,
    },
    peopleRange: {
      start: 10,
      end: 20,
    },
    events: 50,
  },
  {
    amountRange: {
      start: -10,
      end: -20,
    },
    peopleRange: {
      start: 20,
      end: 30,
    },
    events: 40,
  },
  {
    amountRange: {
      start: -10,
      end: -20,
    },
    peopleRange: {
      start: 30,
      end: 40,
    },
    events: 20,
  },
];

const CustomColorRange = () => {
  return (
    <XYChart
      data={numericalDataset}
      colorPalette={[
        { from: 0, to: 30, color: '#109e84' },
        { from: 30, to: 60, color: '#cba261' },
        { from: 60, to: 110, color: '#1861AA' },
      ]}
    >
      <XYChart.RectSeries
        xAxisId="x-first-axis"
        yAxisId="y-first-axis"
        x0Accessor="amountRange.start"
        x1Accessor="amountRange.end"
        y0Accessor="peopleRange.start"
        y1Accessor="peopleRange.end"
        valueAccessor="events"
      />
      <XYChart.XAxis id="x-first-axis" type="numerical" position="bottom" />
      <XYChart.YAxis id="y-first-axis" type="numerical" position="left" />
    </XYChart>
  );
};
```

```tsx
import { XYChart } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';

const numericalDataset = [
  {
    amountRange: {
      start: 0,
      end: -10,
    },
    peopleRange: {
      start: 0,
      end: 10,
    },
    events: 10,
  },

  {
    amountRange: {
      start: 0,
      end: -10,
    },
    peopleRange: {
      start: 10,
      end: 20,
    },
    events: 30,
  },
  {
    amountRange: {
      start: 0,
      end: -10,
    },
    peopleRange: {
      start: 20,
      end: 30,
    },
    events: 50,
  },
  {
    amountRange: {
      start: 0,
      end: -10,
    },
    peopleRange: {
      start: 30,
      end: 40,
    },
    events: 100,
  },
  {
    amountRange: {
      start: -10,
      end: -20,
    },
    peopleRange: {
      start: 0,
      end: 10,
    },
    events: 20,
  },
  {
    amountRange: {
      start: -10,
      end: -20,
    },
    peopleRange: {
      start: 10,
      end: 20,
    },
    events: 50,
  },
  {
    amountRange: {
      start: -10,
      end: -20,
    },
    peopleRange: {
      start: 20,
      end: 30,
    },
    events: 40,
  },
  {
    amountRange: {
      start: -10,
      end: -20,
    },
    peopleRange: {
      start: 30,
      end: 40,
    },
    events: 20,
  },
];

const CustomColorRange = () => {
  return (
    <XYChart
      data={numericalDataset}
      colorPalette={[
        { from: 0, to: 30, color: '#109e84' },
        { from: 30, to: 60, color: '#cba261' },
        { from: 60, to: 110, color: '#1861AA' },
      ]}
    >
      <XYChart.RectSeries
        xAxisId="x-first-axis"
        yAxisId="y-first-axis"
        x0Accessor="amountRange.start"
        x1Accessor="amountRange.end"
        y0Accessor="peopleRange.start"
        y1Accessor="peopleRange.end"
        valueAccessor="events"
      />
      <XYChart.XAxis id="x-first-axis" type="numerical" position="bottom" />
      <XYChart.YAxis id="y-first-axis" type="numerical" position="left" />
    </XYChart>
  );
};
```


#### Size

By default, the chart will use all the available space up to a maximum height of
300 pixels and a width of 100% of the container. This maximum height and width
can be changed by providing a value in the `height` and `width` properties of
the `XYChart`. Both accept a number (treated as pixels) or a CSS string (e.g.,
`"500px"`, `"60%"`).

```tsx
import { XYChart } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';
import { Flex } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/layouts';

const numericalDataset = [
  {
    amountRange: {
      start: 0,
      end: -10,
    },
    peopleRange: {
      start: 0,
      end: 10,
    },
    events: 10,
  },

  {
    amountRange: {
      start: 0,
      end: -10,
    },
    peopleRange: {
      start: 10,
      end: 20,
    },
    events: 30,
  },
  {
    amountRange: {
      start: 0,
      end: -10,
    },
    peopleRange: {
      start: 20,
      end: 30,
    },
    events: 50,
  },
  {
    amountRange: {
      start: 0,
      end: -10,
    },
    peopleRange: {
      start: 30,
      end: 40,
    },
    events: 100,
  },
  {
    amountRange: {
      start: -10,
      end: -20,
    },
    peopleRange: {
      start: 0,
      end: 10,
    },
    events: 20,
  },
  {
    amountRange: {
      start: -10,
      end: -20,
    },
    peopleRange: {
      start: 10,
      end: 20,
    },
    events: 50,
  },
  {
    amountRange: {
      start: -10,
      end: -20,
    },
    peopleRange: {
      start: 20,
      end: 30,
    },
    events: 40,
  },
  {
    amountRange: {
      start: -10,
      end: -20,
    },
    peopleRange: {
      start: 30,
      end: 40,
    },
    events: 20,
  },
];

const ChartSize = () => {
  return (
    <Flex gap={8} flexDirection="column">
      <XYChart data={numericalDataset} height="400px" width={600}>
        <XYChart.RectSeries
          xAxisId="x-first-axis"
          yAxisId="y-first-axis"
          x0Accessor="amountRange.start"
          x1Accessor="amountRange.end"
          y0Accessor="peopleRange.start"
          y1Accessor="peopleRange.end"
          valueAccessor="events"
        />
        <XYChart.XAxis id="x-first-axis" type="numerical" position="bottom" />
        <XYChart.YAxis id="y-first-axis" type="numerical" position="left" />
      </XYChart>
      <XYChart data={numericalDataset} height="400px" width="60%">
        <XYChart.RectSeries
          xAxisId="x-first-axis"
          yAxisId="y-first-axis"
          x0Accessor="amountRange.start"
          x1Accessor="amountRange.end"
          y0Accessor="peopleRange.start"
          y1Accessor="peopleRange.end"
          valueAccessor="events"
        />
        <XYChart.XAxis id="x-first-axis" type="numerical" position="bottom" />
        <XYChart.YAxis id="y-first-axis" type="numerical" position="left" />
      </XYChart>
    </Flex>
  );
};
```

```tsx
const ChartSize = () => {

        <XYChart.RectSeries
          xAxisId="x-first-axis"

          y0Accessor="peopleRange.start"

```


#### Axes

To display a series in the plot, it is necessary to include both `XYChart.XAxis`
and `XYChart.YAxis` subcomponents, which will require an `id` property that
matches the `xAxisId` and `yAxisId` used in a given series.

You will also need to define the `type` property, used to determine the axis
scale type, which can be `numerical`, `log`, `time` or `categorical`, as well as
the `position` to indicate where the axis should be placed, which can be
`bottom`, `top`, `left` or `right`. You can add as many axes as needed to the
chart but only the first axis of each position will be visually displayed.

##### Mirrored Axes

There is an additional position option called `both` for displaying a mirrored
axis for either the `XYChart.XAxis` or the `XYChart.YAxis`.

```tsx

const positiveZeroBaseline = [

const MirroredAxes = () => {
  return (
    <XYChart data={positiveZeroBaseline}>
      <XYChart.RectSeries
        xAxisId="x-mirrored-axis"
        yAxisId="y-mirrored-axis"
        x0Accessor="horizontal.start"
        x1Accessor="horizontal.end"
        y0Accessor="vertical.start"
        y1Accessor="vertical.end"
        valueAccessor="value"
      />
      <XYChart.XAxis id="x-mirrored-axis" type="numerical" position="both" />
      <XYChart.YAxis id="y-mirrored-axis" type="numerical" position="both" />
    </XYChart>
  );
};
```

```tsx
      end: 10,
    },
    value: 20000,
  },
  {
    horizontal: {
      start: 250,
      end: 300,
    },
    vertical: {
      start: 10,
      end: 12,
    },
    value: 30000,
  },
];

const MirroredAxes = () => {
  return (
    <XYChart data={positiveZeroBaseline}>
      <XYChart.RectSeries
        xAxisId="x-mirrored-axis"
        yAxisId="y-mirrored-axis"
        x0Accessor="horizontal.start"
        x1Accessor="horizontal.end"
        y0Accessor="vertical.start"
        y1Accessor="vertical.end"
        valueAccessor="value"
      />
      <XYChart.XAxis id="x-mirrored-axis" type="numerical" position="both" />
      <XYChart.YAxis id="y-mirrored-axis" type="numerical" position="both" />
    </XYChart>
  );
};
```


##### Axis label

The `label` property can be used to include a text label for a given axis. If
set, it will also be shown in the tooltip identifying the corresponding data.

```tsx
const AxisLabel = () => {
  return (
    <XYChart data={randomDateNumber} colorPalette="pink">
      <XYChart.DotSeries
        xAxisId="x-first-axis"
        yAxisId="y-first-axis"
        x0Accessor="aX0"
        y0Accessor="aY0"
        nameAccessor="seriesName"
      />
      <XYChart.DotSeries
        xAxisId="x-first-axis"
        yAxisId="y-first-axis"
        x0Accessor="aX1"
        y0Accessor="aY1"
        nameAccessor="seriesName"
      />
      <XYChart.DotSeries
        xAxisId="x-first-axis"
        yAxisId="y-first-axis"
        x0Accessor="aX2"
        y0Accessor="aY2"
        nameAccessor="seriesName"
      />
      <XYChart.XAxis
        id="x-first-axis"
        type="time"
        position="bottom"
        formatter={dateFormatter}
        label="Bottom Label"
      />
      <XYChart.YAxis
        id="y-first-axis"
        type="numerical"
        position="left"
        label="Left label"
      />
    </XYChart>
  );
};
```

```tsx
const AxisLabel = () => {
  return (
    <XYChart data={randomDateNumber} colorPalette="pink">
      <XYChart.DotSeries
        xAxisId="x-first-axis"
        yAxisId="y-first-axis"
        x0Accessor="aX0"
        y0Accessor="aY0"
        nameAccessor="seriesName"
      />
      <XYChart.DotSeries
        xAxisId="x-first-axis"
        yAxisId="y-first-axis"
        x0Accessor="aX1"
        y0Accessor="aY1"
        nameAccessor="seriesName"
      />
      <XYChart.DotSeries
        xAxisId="x-first-axis"
        yAxisId="y-first-axis"
        x0Accessor="aX2"
        y0Accessor="aY2"
        nameAccessor="seriesName"
      />
      <XYChart.XAxis
        id="x-first-axis"
        type="time"
        position="bottom"
        formatter={dateFormatter}
        label="Bottom Label"
      />
      <XYChart.YAxis
        id="y-first-axis"
        type="numerical"
        position="left"
        label="Left label"
      />
    </XYChart>
  );
};
```


##### Reversed Axis

The `reversed` property inverts the scale on the chosen axis.

```tsx
<XYChart data={numericalDataset}>
  <XYChart.RectSeries
    xAxisId="x-first-axis"
    yAxisId="y-first-axis"
    x0Accessor="amountRange.start"
    x1Accessor="amountRange.end"
    y0Accessor="peopleRange.start"
    y1Accessor="peopleRange.end"
    valueAccessor="events"
  />
  <XYChart.XAxis
    id="x-first-axis"
    type="numerical"
    position="bottom"
    reversed
  />
  <XYChart.YAxis id="y-first-axis" type="numerical" position="left" reversed />
</XYChart>
```

```tsx
<XYChart data={numericalDataset}>
  <XYChart.RectSeries
    xAxisId="x-first-axis"
    yAxisId="y-first-axis"
    x0Accessor="amountRange.start"
    x1Accessor="amountRange.end"
    y0Accessor="peopleRange.start"
    y1Accessor="peopleRange.end"
    valueAccessor="events"
  />
  <XYChart.XAxis
    id="x-first-axis"
    type="numerical"
    position="bottom"
    reversed
  />
  <XYChart.YAxis id="y-first-axis" type="numerical" position="left" reversed />
</XYChart>
```

The `hidden` property can be used to visually hide the axis.

```tsx
        xAxisId="x-first-axis"
        yAxisId="y-first-axis"
```

##### Axis layout and size

The `tickLabelLayout` property gives you the option to choose between a
`horizontal` (default) or `vertical` layout for the axis tick values.

The `tickLabelMaxSize` TBD.

##### Axis min and max

The `min` and `max` properties define the axis boundaries. By default, the
`auto` setting is applied to both `min` and `max` properties, which
automatically calculates the axis scale's minimum and maximum values from the
chart data, akin to `data-min` and `data-max`, and establishes the baseline of
the Y-axis at zero. A custom number or date can also be provided to set custom
axis boundaries.

```tsx
<XYChart data={positiveZeroBaseline}>
  <XYChart.RectSeries
    xAxisId="x-first-axis"
    yAxisId="y-first-axis"
    x0Accessor="horizontal.start"
    x1Accessor="horizontal.end"
    y0Accessor="vertical.start"
    y1Accessor="vertical.end"
    valueAccessor="value"
  />
  <XYChart.XAxis
    id="x-first-axis"
    type="numerical"
    position="bottom"
    min={50}
    max={250}
  />
  <XYChart.YAxis
    id="y-first-axis"
    type="numerical"
    position="left"
    min={2}
    max={10}
  />
</XYChart>
```

```tsx
<XYChart data={positiveZeroBaseline}>
  <XYChart.RectSeries
    xAxisId="x-first-axis"
    yAxisId="y-first-axis"
    x0Accessor="horizontal.start"
    x1Accessor="horizontal.end"
    y0Accessor="vertical.start"
    y1Accessor="vertical.end"
    valueAccessor="value"
  />
  <XYChart.XAxis
    id="x-first-axis"
    type="numerical"
    position="bottom"
    min={50}
    max={250}
  />
  <XYChart.YAxis
    id="y-first-axis"
    type="numerical"
    position="left"
    min={2}
    max={10}
  />
</XYChart>
```


##### Axis unit and formatter

With the `unit` property you can provide a unit that will be displayed along the
data in the chart. The `formatter` will allow you to format data of type date or
number.

```tsx
      />
    </XYChart>

};
```

##### Gap policy

Gaps in data refer to missing or unrepresented values between existing data
points. In a dataset exclusively with timestamps, no continuity can be
determined and therefore there are always “gaps” between data points regardless
of the resolution of the dataset. In a timeframe dataset however, gaps are
considered when the end timestamp of the previous data point is earlier than the
start timestamp of the next data point, meaning there is a timespan between the
end of a previous data point and the next one with no given value. This concept
can also be applied for any type of continuous numerical domain (not only time).

The XYChart axes provide the `gapPolicy` prop to configure how gaps are
visualized in a chart. There are three `gapPolicy` options available: `gap`,
`connect`, and `threshold`.

- The `gap` option displays gaps on the chart as is (e.g. breaks along a line).
This option helps to see the absence of data during a period of time or number
unit.

- The `connect` option connects consecutive data points irrespective of the gap
using linear interpolation.

- The `threshold` option provides a mechanism to configure thresholds of a
certain time or number (e.g. 30 seconds, 5 minutes, 1 day, 3 months, etc. or
positive integers). When the gap between sequential data points in the dataset
is less than or equal to the threshold, then the data points will be
connected.

Note: The `gapPolicy` prop is only supported in line chart variants. The other
variants do not support a gap policy and do not react to the property
configuration for this axis.

```tsx
import { XYChart } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';

const timestampXandNumericalYThreshold = [
  { x: '2024-04-10T08:05:00', y: 10, seriesId: '1', name: 'Threshold 5min' },
  { x: '2024-04-10T08:15:00', y: 25, seriesId: '1', name: 'Threshold 5min' },
  { x: '2024-04-10T08:15:00', y: 30, seriesId: '1', name: 'Threshold 5min' },
  { x: '2024-04-10T08:25:00', y: 45, seriesId: '1', name: 'Threshold 5min' },
  { x: '2024-04-10T08:30:00', y: 75, seriesId: '1', name: 'Threshold 5min' },
  { x: '2024-04-10T08:35:00', y: 60, seriesId: '1', name: 'Threshold 5min' },
  { x: '2024-04-10T08:45:00', y: 125, seriesId: '1', name: 'Threshold 5min' },
  { x: '2024-04-10T08:55:00', y: 90, seriesId: '1', name: 'Threshold 5min' },
  { x: '2024-04-10T09:00:00', y: 175, seriesId: '1', name: 'Threshold 5min' },
  { x: '2024-04-10T09:05:00', y: 120, seriesId: '1', name: 'Threshold 5min' },
  { x: '2024-04-10T09:15:00', y: 140, seriesId: '1', name: 'Threshold 5min' },
  { x: '2024-04-10T09:15:00', y: 225, seriesId: '1', name: 'Threshold 5min' },
  { x: '2024-04-10T09:25:00', y: 160, seriesId: '1', name: 'Threshold 5min' },
  { x: '2024-04-10T09:30:00', y: 275, seriesId: '1', name: 'Threshold 5min' },
  { x: '2024-04-10T09:35:00', y: 180, seriesId: '1', name: 'Threshold 5min' },
  { x: '2024-04-10T09:45:00', y: 220, seriesId: '1', name: 'Threshold 5min' },
  { x: '2024-04-10T09:55:00', y: 240, seriesId: '1', name: 'Threshold 5min' },
];

const numericalAndTimeframeDataConnect = [
  {
    x0: '2023-01-01',
    x1: '2023-01-02',
    y1: 35.678,
    seriesId: '2',
    name: 'Threshold 2d',
  },

  },
  {

  },
  {
    x0: '2023-01-12',

    seriesId: '2',
    name: 'Threshold 2d',
  },
  {
    x0: '2023-01-13',

    seriesId: '2',
    name: 'Threshold 2d',
  },
  {
    x0: '2023-01-14',

    seriesId: '2',
    name: 'Threshold 2d',
  },
  {
    x0: '2023-01-15',

  },
  {
    x0: '2023-01-16',

  },

const GapPolicy = () => {
  return (
    <XYChart data={[]} height="200px" colorPalette="swamps">
      <XYChart.LineSeries
        data={timestampXandNumericalYThreshold}
        xAxisId="x-bottom-axis-threshold"
        yAxisId="y-left-axis"
        x0Accessor="x"
        y0Accessor="y"
        nameAccessor="name"
        seriesIdAccessor="seriesId"
      />
      <XYChart.LineSeries
        data={numericalAndTimeframeDataConnect}
        xAxisId="x-top-axis-connect"
        yAxisId="y-left-axis"
        x0Accessor="x0"
        x1Accessor="x1"
        y0Accessor="y1"
        nameAccessor="name"
        seriesIdAccessor="seriesId"
      />
      <XYChart.XAxis
        id="x-bottom-axis-threshold"
        type="time"
        position="bottom"
        gapPolicy={{ threshold: '5min' }}
      />
      <XYChart.XAxis
        id="x-top-axis-connect"
        type="time"
        position="top"
        gapPolicy={{ threshold: '2d' }}
      />
      <XYChart.YAxis id="y-left-axis" type="numerical" position="left" />
      <XYChart.Toolbar placement="top-left" />
    </XYChart>
  );
};
```

```tsx
const GapPolicy = () => {
  return (
    <XYChart data={[]} height="200px" colorPalette="swamps">
      <XYChart.LineSeries
        data={timestampXandNumericalYThreshold}
        xAxisId="x-bottom-axis-threshold"
        yAxisId="y-left-axis"
        x0Accessor="x"
        y0Accessor="y"
        nameAccessor="name"
        seriesIdAccessor="seriesId"
      />
      <XYChart.LineSeries
        data={numericalAndTimeframeDataConnect}
        xAxisId="x-top-axis-connect"
        yAxisId="y-left-axis"
        x0Accessor="x0"
        x1Accessor="x1"
        y0Accessor="y1"
        nameAccessor="name"
        seriesIdAccessor="seriesId"
      />
      <XYChart.XAxis
        id="x-bottom-axis-threshold"
        type="time"
        position="bottom"
        gapPolicy={{ threshold: '5min' }}
      />
      <XYChart.XAxis
        id="x-top-axis-connect"
        type="time"
        position="top"
        gapPolicy={{ threshold: '2d' }}
      />
      <XYChart.YAxis id="y-left-axis" type="numerical" position="left" />
      <XYChart.Toolbar placement="top-left" />
    </XYChart>
  );
};
```


##### Multiple axes

When using any `XYChart` up to four different axes can be visually displayed at
the same time.

```tsx
const MultipleAxes = () => {
  return (
    <XYChart data={[]} height="300px">
      <XYChart.DotSeries
        data={randomNumbersCategories}
        xAxisId="x-first-axis"
        yAxisId="y-first-axis"
        x0Accessor="x0"
        y0Accessor="y0"
        nameAccessor="seriesName"
        color="green"
      />
      <XYChart.DotSeries
        data={randomNumbersCategories}
        xAxisId="x-first-axis"
        yAxisId="y-first-axis"
        x0Accessor="x1"
        y0Accessor="y1"
        nameAccessor="seriesName"
        color="lime"
      />
      <XYChart.DotSeries
        data={numericalAndTimeData}
        xAxisId="x-second-axis"
        yAxisId="y-second-axis"
        x0Accessor="x"
        y0Accessor="y"
        nameAccessor="seriesName"
        color="#c4233b"
      />
      <XYChart.DotSeries
        data={numericalAndTimeData}
        xAxisId="x-second-axis"
        yAxisId="y-second-axis"
        x0Accessor="x0"
        y0Accessor="y1"
        nameAccessor="seriesName"
        color="#a0001f"
      />
      <XYChart.XAxis id="x-first-axis" type="categorical" position="bottom" />
      <XYChart.YAxis id="y-first-axis" type="numerical" position="left" />
      <XYChart.XAxis id="x-second-axis" type="time" position="top" />
      <XYChart.YAxis id="y-second-axis" type="numerical" position="right" />
    </XYChart>
  );
};
```

```tsx
const MultipleAxes = () => {
  return (
    <XYChart data={[]} height="300px">
      <XYChart.DotSeries
        data={randomNumbersCategories}
        xAxisId="x-first-axis"
        yAxisId="y-first-axis"
        x0Accessor="x0"
        y0Accessor="y0"
        nameAccessor="seriesName"
        color="green"
      />
      <XYChart.DotSeries
        data={randomNumbersCategories}
        xAxisId="x-first-axis"
        yAxisId="y-first-axis"
        x0Accessor="x1"
        y0Accessor="y1"
        nameAccessor="seriesName"
        color="lime"
      />
      <XYChart.DotSeries
        data={numericalAndTimeData}
        xAxisId="x-second-axis"
        yAxisId="y-second-axis"
        x0Accessor="x"
        y0Accessor="y"
        nameAccessor="seriesName"
        color="#c4233b"
      />
      <XYChart.DotSeries
        data={numericalAndTimeData}
        xAxisId="x-second-axis"
        yAxisId="y-second-axis"
        x0Accessor="x0"
        y0Accessor="y1"
        nameAccessor="seriesName"
        color="#a0001f"
      />
      <XYChart.XAxis id="x-first-axis" type="categorical" position="bottom" />
      <XYChart.YAxis id="y-first-axis" type="numerical" position="left" />
      <XYChart.XAxis id="x-second-axis" type="time" position="top" />
      <XYChart.YAxis id="y-second-axis" type="numerical" position="right" />
    </XYChart>
  );
};
```


#### Legend

The purpose of the legend is to provide additional identifying information for
the chart, without needing to interact with the legend directly. If there are
multiple series in the chart, only the first series will be reflected on the
legend.

##### Visibility

The legend of the `XYChart` will be displayed by default, but can be optionally
hidden by setting the `hidden` property to true on the `XYChart.Legend`
subcomponent.

##### Position

By default, the position of the legend of the `XYChart` is set automatically to
the right of the chart area. When the chart width is reduced, the legend is
repositioned to the area beneath the chart. It is also possible to explicitly
set the chart's legend position to right or bottom with the `position` prop.

```tsx
import { XYChart } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';

const stdData = [
  { seriesName: 'First Series', x0: 26.83, y0: 'Vienna' },
  { seriesName: 'First Series', x0: 31.4, y0: 'Rome' },

  { seriesName: 'First Series', x0: 42.05, y0: 'Vienna' },
  { seriesName: 'First Series', x0: 37.72, y0: 'Vienna' },
  { seriesName: 'First Series', x0: 15.34, y0: 'Paris' },
  { seriesName: 'First Series', x0: 40.38, y0: 'London' },

const LegendPosition = () => {
  return (
    <XYChart data={stdData} colorPalette="swamps">
      <XYChart.DotSeries
        xAxisId="x-axis"
        yAxisId="y-axis"
        x0Accessor="x0"
        y0Accessor="y0"
        nameAccessor="seriesName"
      />
      <XYChart.DotSeries
        xAxisId="x-axis"
        yAxisId="y-axis"
        x0Accessor="x1"
        y0Accessor="y1"
        nameAccessor="seriesName"
      />
      <XYChart.DotSeries
        xAxisId="x-axis"
        yAxisId="y-axis"
        x0Accessor="x2"
        y0Accessor="y2"
        nameAccessor="seriesName"
      />
      <XYChart.DotSeries
        xAxisId="x-axis"
        yAxisId="y-axis"
        x0Accessor="x3"
        y0Accessor="y3"
        nameAccessor="seriesName"
      />
      <XYChart.XAxis id="x-axis" type="numerical" position="bottom" />
      <XYChart.YAxis id="y-axis" type="categorical" position="left" />
      <XYChart.Legend position="bottom" />
    </XYChart>
  );
};
```
