```tsx
  );
};
```


### Props

The `XYChart` encompasses various types of charts that utilize both x-axis and
y-axis for data representation, designed to visually display and analyze data
that involve two numerical variables.

OverviewProperties

#### XYChartProps
extends`, , ` |
 | Name | Type | Default | Description
 | `data` | [] | | Data object for the xy chart.
 | `height?` | | | `300px` | Chart height. When a number is specified, it's treated as pixels,
otherwise a valid height string is expected.
 | `width?` | | | `100%` | Chart width. When a number is specified, it's treated as pixels,
otherwise a valid width string is expected.
 | `colorPalette?` | | `'blue'` | Color palette for the xy chart.
 | `loading?` | | `false` | Show the loading indicator when truthy.
 | `truncationMode?` | | `'middle'` | Truncation mode to be used as start, middle or end, and applied to all the parts that truncate text.
 | `__downloadDataCallback?` | () => | | Callback invoked when a download is triggered. Receives the CSV string
so the caller can drive the actual download (e.g. for migration purposes).
When provided, the default browser download is skipped.

#### XYChart.XAxis

To configure the x-axis, add `XYChart.XAxis` to the xy chart.

#### XYChartXAxisProps

##### Signature:
`export declare type XYChartXAxisProps = & {
 /**
 * Whether the tick labels are displayed in a or layout.
 * @defaultValue
 */
 tickLabelLayout?: ;
 /** The position for the xy chart x-axis */
 position: ;
 /** If true, the zoom interaction will be disabled
 * @defaultValue false
 */
 disableZoom?: ;
 /** If true, the pan interaction will be disabled
 * @defaultValue false
 */
 disablePan?: ;
 /** Sets the new zoom and pan domain boundaries, overwriting the ones that come from the data */
 initialZoom?: [, ] | [, ];
 /** Sets the domain of the axis to start at the specified values */
 currentZoom?: [, ] | [, ];
 /** If true, the chart will be able to zoom and pan outside the set boundaries
 * @defaultValue false
 */
 infiniteZoom?: ;
 /** Handler triggered when there is a change on the time domain caused by a zoom action */
 onZoomChange?: ;
};`

#### XYChart.YAxis

To configure the y-axis, add `XYChart.YAxis` to the xy chart.

#### XYChartYAxisProps

##### Signature:
`export declare type XYChartYAxisProps = & {
 position: ;
};`

#### XYChart.Legend

To configure the legend, add `XYChart.Legend` to the xy chart.

#### XYChartChartLegendProps

##### Signature:
`export declare type XYChartChart = ;`

#### XYChart.Toolbar

To customise the toolbar, add `XYChart.Toolbar` to the xy chart.

#### XYChartToolbarProps

##### Signature:
`export declare type XYChartToolbarProps = & {
 /** Decides if the toolbar is visible or hidden
 * @defaultValue false
 */
 hidden?: ;
};`

#### XYChart.EmptyState

`XYChart.EmptyState` provides a slot where the Empty state wrapper can be set.

#### EmptyStateProp
 |
 | Name | Type | Default | Description
 | `children` | | |

#### XYChart.ErrorState

`XYChart.ErrorState` provides a slot where the Error state wrapper can be set.

#### ErrorStateProps
 |
 | Name | Type | Default | Description
 | `children` | | ((errorMessage: ) => .) | |

#### XYChart.RectSeries

`XYChart.RectSeries` provides a slot for defining a `RectSeries`.

#### XYChartRectSeriesProps
extends |
 | Name | Type | Default | Description
 | `x1Accessor?` | | | Accessor function to retrieve the data for the x1.
 | `y1Accessor?` | | | Accessor function to retrieve the data for the y1.
 | `valueAccessor` | | | Accessor function to retrieve the data for the value.
 | `valueAccessorLabel?` | | | A custom label to overwrite the valueAccessor when naming the value
 | `valueMin?` | | | If specified, all values coming from the valueAccessor that are under this number will display a default color.
Doesn't apply for category values.
 | `valueMax?` | | | If specified, all values coming from the valueAccessor that are above this number will display a default color.
Doesn't apply for category values.
 | `valueFormatter?` | | | | If specified, the value coming from the valueAccessor will be formatted with given formatter options.
Doesn't apply for string values.
 | `valueUnit?` | | | A custom unit to be displayed in the Legend for number values
 | `actions?` | (datapoint: , series?: ) => | | Custom actions to be shown in the tooltip and legend.
For tooltip, information about the selected datapoint is displayed.
For legend, the first parameter returns the first datapoint of the series and the second parameter returns the series object.

#### XYChart.DotSeries

`XYChart.DotSeries` provides a slot for defining a `DotSeries`.

#### XYChartDotSeriesProps

##### Signature:
`export declare type Props = & & {
 /**
 * Series name.
 * @deprecated - Use nameAccessor instead. This key will be removed in a future release.
 */
 name?: ;
 /**
 * Accessor function to retrieve the data for the x1.
 */
 x1Accessor?: ;
 /**
 * Accessor function to retrieve the data for the y1.
 */
 y1Accessor?: ;
 /**
 * Series ID accessor.
 */
 seriesIdAccessor?: ;
 /**
 * Series name accessor.
 */
 nameAccessor?: ;
 /**
 * Custom actions to be shown in the tooltip and legend.
 * For tooltip, information about the selected datapoint is displayed.
 * For legend, the first parameter returns the first datapoint of the series and the second parameter returns the series object.
 */
 actions?: (datapoint: , series?: ) => ;
 /**
 * Determines the visual appearance of the dot, such as , , , or .
 * @defaultValue
 */
 shape?: ;
};`

#### XYChart.LineSeries

`XYChart.LineSeries` provides a slot for defining a `LineSeries`.

#### XYChartLineSeriesProps

##### Signature:
`export declare type Props = & & {
 /**
 * Accessor function to retrieve the data for the x1.
 */
 x1Accessor?: ;
 /**
 * Accessor function to retrieve the data for the y1.
 */
 y1Accessor?: ;
 /**
 * Series ID accessor.
 */
 seriesIdAccessor: ;
 /**
 * Series name accessor.
 */
 nameAccessor: ;
 /**
 * Custom actions to be shown in the tooltip and legend.
 * For tooltip, information about the selected datapoint is displayed.
 * For legend, the first parameter returns the first datapoint of the series and the second parameter returns the series object.
 */
 actions?: (datapoint: , series?: ) => ;
 /**

};`

### XYChart.Annotations

`XYChart.Annotations` provides a slot for defining annotations in the chart.

#### XYChartAnnotationsProps
extends |
 | Name | Type | Default | Description
 | `data` | [] | | Data object for the xy chart.
 | `xAxisId` | | | Unique Id required to attach the annotations to its x-axis.
 | `showLabels?` | | `false` | Flag for showing/hiding track labels
 | `fixedTracks?` | | `3` | Amount of tracks visible without scrolling.
 | `annotationsActions?` | | | Custom annotations actions on entry selection.

### XYChartAnnotations.Track

`XYChartAnnotations.Track` provides a slot for defining a track in the
`XYChart.Annotations`.

#### XYChartAnnotationsMarkerProps
 |
 | Name | Type | Default | Description
 | `startAccessor` | | | Indicates what data field from the data object should be used as the start point for the markers
 | `endAccessor` | | | Indicates what data field from the data object should be used as the end point of the markers
 | `trackByAccessor` | | | Indicates what data field from the data object should be used to link this marker to a track. It must contain the unique id of a track.
 | `descriptionAccessor?` | | | Indicates what data field from the data object should be used as the description of each marker
 | `colorAccessor?` | | `Colors.Charts.Categorical.Color01.Default` | Indicates what data field from the data object should be used as the color of each marker
 | `titleAccessor?` | | | Indicates what data field from the data object should be used as the title of each marker
 | `symbol?` | | ((t: ) => ) | | letter/emoji/icon/glyph or Unicode character
 | `indicatorsDisplay?` | | ((t: ) => ) | | Defines the display behaviour of marker's indicators of a particular track
 | `hidden?` | | ((t: ) => ) | | Flag to hide a marker
 | `priority?` | | ((t: ) => ) | | Priority of annotation in case of overlapping. The higher the number, the higher priority

## XYChart-AreaSeries

`/design/data-visualizations/charts/XYChart-AreaSeries/`

## AreaSeries
An area series chart fills the region between data points and a baseline, making
it effective for visualizing magnitudes, trends, and cumulative values over a
continuous range. It combines line-based trend visibility with filled regions to
emphasize volume, helping to reveal patterns and comparisons across series.

OverviewProperties

### Import

`tsx
import { XYChart } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';
`

### Overview

To better understand the accessor patterns, here are some common terms used in
this documentation:

- Primary Accessor: Defines the area's position along the main axis (e.g.,
`x0Accessor` for vertical areas).

- Growth Accessor: Defines the area's upper boundary (e.g., `y1Accessor` for
vertical areas).

- Baseline Accessor: Defines the area's lower boundary (e.g., `y0Accessor`
for vertical bands). When omitted, the area fills from the growth value down
to the axis.

- Primary Axis: The axis associated with the primary accessor.

- Growth Axis: The axis associated with the growth accessor.

```tsx
import { XYChart } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';

const data = [
  { x: '2024-01-01', y: 42, seriesId: 'frontend', name: 'Frontend' },
  { x: '2024-01-02', y: 38, seriesId: 'frontend', name: 'Frontend' },
  { x: '2024-01-03', y: 65, seriesId: 'frontend', name: 'Frontend' },
  { x: '2024-01-04', y: 78, seriesId: 'frontend', name: 'Frontend' },
  { x: '2024-01-05', y: 92, seriesId: 'frontend', name: 'Frontend' },
  { x: '2024-01-06', y: 71, seriesId: 'frontend', name: 'Frontend' },
  { x: '2024-01-07', y: 55, seriesId: 'frontend', name: 'Frontend' },
  { x: '2024-01-08', y: 48, seriesId: 'frontend', name: 'Frontend' },
  { x: '2024-01-09', y: 62, seriesId: 'frontend', name: 'Frontend' },
  { x: '2024-01-10', y: 85, seriesId: 'frontend', name: 'Frontend' },
  { x: '2024-01-01', y: 25, seriesId: 'backend', name: 'Backend' },
  { x: '2024-01-02', y: 28, seriesId: 'backend', name: 'Backend' },
  { x: '2024-01-03', y: 32, seriesId: 'backend', name: 'Backend' },
  { x: '2024-01-04', y: 38, seriesId: 'backend', name: 'Backend' },
  { x: '2024-01-05', y: 45, seriesId: 'backend', name: 'Backend' },
  { x: '2024-01-06', y: 42, seriesId: 'backend', name: 'Backend' },
  { x: '2024-01-07', y: 50, seriesId: 'backend', name: 'Backend' },
  { x: '2024-01-08', y: 58, seriesId: 'backend', name: 'Backend' },
  { x: '2024-01-09', y: 65, seriesId: 'backend', name: 'Backend' },
  { x: '2024-01-10', y: 70, seriesId: 'backend', name: 'Backend' },
  { x: '2024-01-01', y: 15, seriesId: 'database', name: 'Database' },
  { x: '2024-01-02', y: 22, seriesId: 'database', name: 'Database' },
  { x: '2024-01-03', y: 28, seriesId: 'database', name: 'Database' },
  { x: '2024-01-04', y: 32, seriesId: 'database', name: 'Database' },
  { x: '2024-01-05', y: 30, seriesId: 'database', name: 'Database' },
  { x: '2024-01-06', y: 24, seriesId: 'database', name: 'Database' },
  { x: '2024-01-07', y: 20, seriesId: 'database', name: 'Database' },
  { x: '2024-01-08', y: 25, seriesId: 'database', name: 'Database' },
  { x: '2024-01-09', y: 33, seriesId: 'database', name: 'Database' },
  { x: '2024-01-10', y: 38, seriesId: 'database', name: 'Database' },
  { x: '2024-01-01', y: 8, seriesId: 'cache', name: 'Cache' },
  { x: '2024-01-02', y: 12, seriesId: 'cache', name: 'Cache' },
  { x: '2024-01-03', y: 10, seriesId: 'cache', name: 'Cache' },
  { x: '2024-01-04', y: 15, seriesId: 'cache', name: 'Cache' },
  { x: '2024-01-05', y: 18, seriesId: 'cache', name: 'Cache' },
  { x: '2024-01-06', y: 21, seriesId: 'cache', name: 'Cache' },
  { x: '2024-01-07', y: 16, seriesId: 'cache', name: 'Cache' },
  { x: '2024-01-08', y: 20, seriesId: 'cache', name: 'Cache' },
  { x: '2024-01-09', y: 25, seriesId: 'cache', name: 'Cache' },
  { x: '2024-01-10', y: 15, seriesId: 'cache', name: 'Cache' },
];

const AreaSeriesSimple = () => {
  return (
    <XYChart data={[]} height="200px">
      <XYChart.AreaSeries
        data={data}
        xAxisId="x-axis"
        yAxisId="y-axis"
        x0Accessor="x"
        y1Accessor="y"
        seriesIdAccessor="seriesId"
        nameAccessor="name"
      />
      <XYChart.XAxis
        id="x-axis"
        type="time"
        position="bottom"
        gapPolicy="connect"
      />
      <XYChart.YAxis id="y-axis" type="numerical" position="left" />
    </XYChart>
  );
};
```



#### Area vs Band

The AreaSeries can render in two visual modes depending on the accessor pattern:

- Area: When only a growth accessor is provided (no baseline accessor), the
filled region extends from the data line down to the axis origin. This is the
standard area chart.

- Band: When both a growth accessor and a baseline accessor are provided
(e.g., `y0Accessor` and `y1Accessor`), the filled region spans between the two
values. This is useful for confidence intervals, min-max ranges, or tolerance
bands.

The following example shows the same data rendered as an area (no baseline) and
as a band (with a baseline). When `y0Accessor` is omitted the filled region
extends to the axis; when it is provided the fill spans between `y0` and `y1`.

```tsx
import { XYChart } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';
import { Flex } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/layouts';

const data = [
  { x: '2024-01-01', lower: 18, upper: 42, seriesId: 'cpu', name: 'CPU Usage' },
  { x: '2024-01-02', lower: 22, upper: 48, seriesId: 'cpu', name: 'CPU Usage' },
  { x: '2024-01-03', lower: 28, upper: 62, seriesId: 'cpu', name: 'CPU Usage' },

const AreaVsBand = () => {
  return (
    <Flex flexWrap="wrap">
      <Flex flexItem width="48%">
        <h5>Area (no baseline — fills to axis)</h5>
        <XYChart data={[]} height="200px">
          <XYChart.AreaSeries
            data={data}
            xAxisId="x-axis"
            yAxisId="y-axis"
            x0Accessor="x"
            y1Accessor="upper"
            seriesIdAccessor="seriesId"
            nameAccessor="name"
          />
          <XYChart.XAxis
            id="x-axis"
            type="time"
            position="bottom"
            gapPolicy="connect"
          />
          <XYChart.YAxis id="y-axis" type="numerical" position="left" />
        </XYChart>
      </Flex>
      <Flex flexItem width="48%">
        <h5>Band (with baseline — fills between y0 and y1)</h5>
        <XYChart data={[]} height="200px">
          <XYChart.AreaSeries
            data={data}
            xAxisId="x-axis"
            yAxisId="y-axis"
            x0Accessor="x"
            y0Accessor="lower"
            y1Accessor="upper"
            seriesIdAccessor="seriesId"
            nameAccessor="name"
          />
          <XYChart.XAxis
            id="x-axis"
            type="time"
            position="bottom"
            gapPolicy="connect"
          />
          <XYChart.YAxis id="y-axis" type="numerical" position="left" />
        </XYChart>
      </Flex>
    </Flex>
  );
};
```

```tsx
    name: 'Network Latency',
  },
];

const AreaVsBand = () => {
  return (
    <Flex flexWrap="wrap">
      <Flex flexItem width="48%">
        <h5>Area (no baseline — fills to axis)</h5>
        <XYChart data={[]} height="200px">
          <XYChart.AreaSeries
            data={data}
            xAxisId="x-axis"
            yAxisId="y-axis"
            x0Accessor="x"
            y1Accessor="upper"
            seriesIdAccessor="seriesId"
            nameAccessor="name"
          />
          <XYChart.XAxis
            id="x-axis"
            type="time"
            position="bottom"
            gapPolicy="connect"
          />
          <XYChart.YAxis id="y-axis" type="numerical" position="left" />
        </XYChart>
      </Flex>
      <Flex flexItem width="48%">
        <h5>Band (with baseline — fills between y0 and y1)</h5>
        <XYChart data={[]} height="200px">
          <XYChart.AreaSeries
            data={data}
            xAxisId="x-axis"
            yAxisId="y-axis"
            x0Accessor="x"
            y0Accessor="lower"
            y1Accessor="upper"
            seriesIdAccessor="seriesId"
            nameAccessor="name"
          />
          <XYChart.XAxis
            id="x-axis"
            type="time"
            position="bottom"
            gapPolicy="connect"
          />
          <XYChart.YAxis id="y-axis" type="numerical" position="left" />
        </XYChart>
      </Flex>
    </Flex>
  );
};
```


### Usage

Add `XYChart.AreaSeries` inside `XYChart`. Provide accessors to describe the
area's position (primary accessor) and upper boundary (growth accessor).
Orientation is inferred from the accessor pattern.

Required accessors depend on the pattern:

- Vertical area: `x0Accessor` (position) + `y1Accessor` (height)

- Horizontal area: `y0Accessor` (position) + `x1Accessor` (width)

- Vertical band: `x0Accessor` + `y0Accessor` + `y1Accessor`

- Horizontal band: `y0Accessor` + `x0Accessor` + `x1Accessor`

- Vertical area with x-range (timeframe): `x0Accessor` + `x1Accessor` +
`y1Accessor`

- Horizontal area with y-range (timeframe): `y0Accessor` + `y1Accessor` +
`x1Accessor`

Additionally:

- `seriesIdAccessor`: groups datapoints into series (enables multiple series in
one AreaSeries slot)

- `nameAccessor`: series label for legends and tooltips

The `XYChart.AreaSeries` subcomponent also includes the optional properties:
`data`, `color`, `actions`, `pointsDisplay` and `curve`.

Assign the series to axes via `xAxisId` and `yAxisId`. See
XYChart docs for axis configuration.

### Stacking

When multiple series share the same growth axis (numerical, time, or log), the
AreaSeries automatically stacks them. Each area's filled region starts where the
previous one ends.

```tsx
import { XYChart } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';

const data = [
  { x: '2024-01-01', y: 30, seriesId: 'frontend', name: 'Frontend' },
  { x: '2024-01-02', y: 45, seriesId: 'frontend', name: 'Frontend' },
  { x: '2024-01-03', y: 41, seriesId: 'frontend', name: 'Frontend' },
  { x: '2024-01-04', y: 47, seriesId: 'frontend', name: 'Frontend' },
  { x: '2024-01-05', y: 52, seriesId: 'frontend', name: 'Frontend' },
  { x: '2024-01-06', y: 49, seriesId: 'frontend', name: 'Frontend' },
  { x: '2024-01-07', y: 55, seriesId: 'frontend', name: 'Frontend' },
  { x: '2024-01-01', y: 20, seriesId: 'backend', name: 'Backend' },
  { x: '2024-01-02', y: 35, seriesId: 'backend', name: 'Backend' },
  { x: '2024-01-03', y: 28, seriesId: 'backend', name: 'Backend' },
  { x: '2024-01-04', y: 32, seriesId: 'backend', name: 'Backend' },
  { x: '2024-01-05', y: 40, seriesId: 'backend', name: 'Backend' },
  { x: '2024-01-06', y: 38, seriesId: 'backend', name: 'Backend' },
  { x: '2024-01-07', y: 42, seriesId: 'backend', name: 'Backend' },
  { x: '2024-01-01', y: 10, seriesId: 'database', name: 'Database' },
  { x: '2024-01-02', y: 15, seriesId: 'database', name: 'Database' },
  { x: '2024-01-03', y: 12, seriesId: 'database', name: 'Database' },
  { x: '2024-01-04', y: 18, seriesId: 'database', name: 'Database' },
  { x: '2024-01-05', y: 22, seriesId: 'database', name: 'Database' },
  { x: '2024-01-06', y: 20, seriesId: 'database', name: 'Database' },
  { x: '2024-01-07', y: 25, seriesId: 'database', name: 'Database' },
];

const AreaSeriesStacked = () => {
  return (
    <XYChart data={[]} height="200px">
      <XYChart.AreaSeries
        data={data}
        xAxisId="x-axis"
        yAxisId="y-axis"
        x0Accessor="x"
        y1Accessor="y"
        seriesIdAccessor="seriesId"
        nameAccessor="name"
      />
      <XYChart.XAxis
        id="x-axis"
        type="time"
        position="bottom"
        gapPolicy="connect"
      />
      <XYChart.YAxis id="y-axis" type="numerical" position="left" />
    </XYChart>
  );
};
```



### Data

The `data` property can be used to overwrite the `XYChart` component for a
specific series.

#### Custom Tooltip

The XYChart component supports custom tooltips via the `XYChart.CustomTooltip`
subcomponent. This allows you to fully control the content and layout of
tooltips for different series types.

Wrap your custom rendering logic in `XYChart.CustomTooltip` and use the provided
payload to determine which series is being hovered. You can return any JSX, or
null to hide the tooltip for a specific series. If you return undefined, the
default tooltip will be used.

```tsx
<XYChart data={data} height="200px">
  <XYChart.Tooltip>{customTooltipComponent}</XYChart.Tooltip>
  <XYChart.AreaSeries
    xAxisId="x-axis"
    yAxisId="y-axis"
    x0Accessor="x"
    y1Accessor="y"
    nameAccessor="name"
    seriesIdAccessor="seriesId"
  />
  <XYChart.XAxis
    id="x-axis"
    type="time"
    position="bottom"
    gapPolicy="connect"
  />
  <XYChart.YAxis id="y-axis" type="numerical" position="left" />
</XYChart>
```

```tsx
<XYChart data={data} height="200px">
  <XYChart.Tooltip>{customTooltipComponent}</XYChart.Tooltip>
  <XYChart.AreaSeries
    xAxisId="x-axis"
    yAxisId="y-axis"
    x0Accessor="x"
    y1Accessor="y"
    nameAccessor="name"
    seriesIdAccessor="seriesId"
  />
  <XYChart.XAxis
    id="x-axis"
    type="time"
    position="bottom"
    gapPolicy="connect"
  />
  <XYChart.YAxis id="y-axis" type="numerical" position="left" />
</XYChart>
```


#### Points Display

The `pointsDisplay` prop allows you to customize when data points are shown on
the area: `never` hides all points, `always` displays all points, and `auto`
(default) shows points based on a ratio between density of points for the
current series and the chart's width.

```tsx
import { XYChart } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';
import { Flex } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/layouts';

const data = [
  { x: '2024-01-01', y: 25, seriesId: 'requests', name: 'Requests' },
  { x: '2024-01-02', y: 32, seriesId: 'requests', name: 'Requests' },
  { x: '2024-01-03', y: 28, seriesId: 'requests', name: 'Requests' },
  { x: '2024-01-04', y: 38, seriesId: 'requests', name: 'Requests' },
  { x: '2024-01-05', y: 45, seriesId: 'requests', name: 'Requests' },
  { x: '2024-01-06', y: 42, seriesId: 'requests', name: 'Requests' },
  { x: '2024-01-07', y: 35, seriesId: 'requests', name: 'Requests' },
  { x: '2024-01-08', y: 40, seriesId: 'requests', name: 'Requests' },
  { x: '2024-01-09', y: 48, seriesId: 'requests', name: 'Requests' },
  { x: '2024-01-10', y: 52, seriesId: 'requests', name: 'Requests' },
];

const PointsDisplay = () => {
  return (
    <Flex flexDirection="column" gap={16}>
      <Flex flexDirection="row" justifyContent="center">
        <Flex flexDirection="column">
          <h5>Auto (default)</h5>
          <XYChart data={[]} height={180} width={500}>
            <XYChart.AreaSeries
              data={data}
              xAxisId="x-axis"
              yAxisId="y-axis"
              x0Accessor="x"
              y1Accessor="y"
              seriesIdAccessor="seriesId"
              nameAccessor="name"
              pointsDisplay="auto"
            />
            <XYChart.XAxis
              id="x-axis"
              type="time"
              position="bottom"
              gapPolicy="connect"
            />
            <XYChart.YAxis id="y-axis" type="numerical" position="left" />
          </XYChart>
        </Flex>
      </Flex>

      <Flex
        flexDirection="row"
        gap={16}
        justifyContent="center"
        alignItems="center"
      >
        <Flex flexItem flex={1} flexDirection="column">
          <h5>Never</h5>
          <XYChart data={[]} height={180} width={500}>
            <XYChart.AreaSeries
              data={data}
              xAxisId="x-axis"
              yAxisId="y-axis"
              x0Accessor="x"
              y1Accessor="y"
              seriesIdAccessor="seriesId"
              nameAccessor="name"
              pointsDisplay="never"
            />
            <XYChart.XAxis
              id="x-axis"
              type="time"
              position="bottom"
              gapPolicy="connect"
            />
            <XYChart.YAxis id="y-axis" type="numerical" position="left" />
          </XYChart>
        </Flex>
        <Flex
          flexItem
          flex={1}
          justifyContent="center"
          alignItems="center"
          flexDirection="column"
        >
          <h5>Always</h5>
          <XYChart data={[]} height={180} width={200}>
            <XYChart.AreaSeries
              data={data}
              xAxisId="x-axis"
              yAxisId="y-axis"
              x0Accessor="x"
              y1Accessor="y"
              seriesIdAccessor="seriesId"
              nameAccessor="name"
              pointsDisplay="always"
            />
            <XYChart.XAxis
              id="x-axis"
              type="time"
              position="bottom"
              gapPolicy="connect"
            />
            <XYChart.YAxis id="y-axis" type="numerical" position="left" />
          </XYChart>
        </Flex>
      </Flex>
    </Flex>
  );
};
```

#### Value representation

Relative values indicate the proportion that a given dimension contributes to
the total. The `valueRepresentation` prop on the axis can be used to switch
between absolute and relative display.

- For single areas and band configurations, the relative value is based on the
maximum absolute value within the series.

- For stacked areas, each area will scale relative to the total stack height at
each point, so the full height represents 100%. In case of having positive and
negative values, they are scaled together where 100% is the highest absolute
value amongst the two parts.

```tsx
import { XYChart } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';
import { Flex } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/layouts';

const data = [
  { x: 0, y: 10, seriesId: 'series', name: 'Series 1' },
  { x: 10, y: 25, seriesId: 'series', name: 'Series 1' },
  { x: 20, y: 50, seriesId: 'series', name: 'Series 1' },
  { x: 30, y: 80, seriesId: 'series', name: 'Series 1' },
  { x: 40, y: 105, seriesId: 'series', name: 'Series 1' },
  { x: 50, y: 120, seriesId: 'series', name: 'Series 1' },
  { x: 60, y: 125, seriesId: 'series', name: 'Series 1' },
  { x: 70, y: 115, seriesId: 'series', name: 'Series 1' },
  { x: 80, y: 90, seriesId: 'series', name: 'Series 1' },
  { x: 90, y: 55, seriesId: 'series', name: 'Series 1' },
];

const ValueRepresentationSingleArea = () => {
  return (
    <Flex flexWrap="wrap">
      <Flex flexItem width="48%">
        <h5>Absolute Value Representation</h5>
        <XYChart data={[]} height="200px">
          <XYChart.AreaSeries
            data={data}
            xAxisId="x-axis"
            yAxisId="y-axis"
            x0Accessor="x"
            y1Accessor="y"
            seriesIdAccessor="seriesId"
            nameAccessor="name"
          />
          <XYChart.XAxis
            id="x-axis"
            type="numerical"
            position="bottom"
            valueRepresentation="absolute"
          />
          <XYChart.YAxis
            id="y-axis"
            type="numerical"
            position="left"
            valueRepresentation="absolute"
          />
        </XYChart>
      </Flex>
      <Flex flexItem width="48%">
        <h5>Relative Value Representation</h5>
        <XYChart data={[]} height="200px">
          <XYChart.AreaSeries
            data={data}
            xAxisId="x-axis"
            yAxisId="y-axis"
            x0Accessor="x"
            y1Accessor="y"
            seriesIdAccessor="seriesId"
            nameAccessor="name"
          />
          <XYChart.XAxis
            id="x-axis"
            type="numerical"
            position="bottom"
            valueRepresentation="relative"
          />
          <XYChart.YAxis
            id="y-axis"
            type="numerical"
            position="left"
            valueRepresentation="relative"
          />
        </XYChart>
      </Flex>
    </Flex>
  );
};
```

#### Series curve

You can select the curve shape of the `AreaSeries` via the `curve` prop. The
available options are `linear` where line joins will be straight (default
option) or `smooth` for a more fluid curve.

```tsx
import { XYChart } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';

const smoothData = [
  { x: 5, y0: 5, y1: 18, seriesId: '1', name: 'Smooth (band)' },
  { x: 10, y0: 8, y1: 22, seriesId: '1', name: 'Smooth (band)' },
  { x: 15, y0: 4, y1: 16, seriesId: '1', name: 'Smooth (band)' },
  { x: 20, y0: 10, y1: 25, seriesId: '1', name: 'Smooth (band)' },
  { x: 25, y0: 6, y1: 19, seriesId: '1', name: 'Smooth (band)' },
  { x: 30, y0: 12, y1: 27, seriesId: '1', name: 'Smooth (band)' },
];

const linearData = [
  { x: 35, y: 15, seriesId: '2', name: 'Linear (default)' },
  { x: 40, y: 23, seriesId: '2', name: 'Linear (default)' },
  { x: 45, y: 17, seriesId: '2', name: 'Linear (default)' },
  { x: 50, y: 25, seriesId: '2', name: 'Linear (default)' },
  { x: 55, y: 19, seriesId: '2', name: 'Linear (default)' },
  { x: 60, y: 27, seriesId: '2', name: 'Linear (default)' },
];

const SeriesCurve = () => {
  return (
    <XYChart data={[]} height="200px" colorPalette="fireplace">
      <XYChart.AreaSeries
        data={linearData}
        xAxisId="x-axis"
        yAxisId="y-axis"
        x0Accessor="x"
        y1Accessor="y"
        seriesIdAccessor="seriesId"
        nameAccessor="name"
      />
      <XYChart.AreaSeries
        data={smoothData}
        xAxisId="x-axis"
        yAxisId="y-axis"
        x0Accessor="x"
        y0Accessor="y0"
        y1Accessor="y1"
        seriesIdAccessor="seriesId"
        nameAccessor="name"
        pointsDisplay="always"
        curve="smooth"
      />
      <XYChart.XAxis id="x-axis" type="numerical" position="bottom" max={65} />
      <XYChart.YAxis id="y-axis" type="numerical" position="left" max={35} />
    </XYChart>
  );
};
```

```tsx
import { XYChart } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';

const smoothData = [
  { x: 5, y0: 5, y1: 18, seriesId: '1', name: 'Smooth (band)' },
  { x: 10, y0: 8, y1: 22, seriesId: '1', name: 'Smooth (band)' },
  { x: 15, y0: 4, y1: 16, seriesId: '1', name: 'Smooth (band)' },
  { x: 20, y0: 10, y1: 25, seriesId: '1', name: 'Smooth (band)' },
  { x: 25, y0: 6, y1: 19, seriesId: '1', name: 'Smooth (band)' },
  { x: 30, y0: 12, y1: 27, seriesId: '1', name: 'Smooth (band)' },
];

const linearData = [
  { x: 35, y: 15, seriesId: '2', name: 'Linear (default)' },
  { x: 40, y: 23, seriesId: '2', name: 'Linear (default)' },
  { x: 45, y: 17, seriesId: '2', name: 'Linear (default)' },
  { x: 50, y: 25, seriesId: '2', name: 'Linear (default)' },
  { x: 55, y: 19, seriesId: '2', name: 'Linear (default)' },
  { x: 60, y: 27, seriesId: '2', name: 'Linear (default)' },
];

const SeriesCurve = () => {
  return (
    <XYChart data={[]} height="200px" colorPalette="fireplace">
      <XYChart.AreaSeries
        data={linearData}
        xAxisId="x-axis"
        yAxisId="y-axis"
        x0Accessor="x"
        y1Accessor="y"
        seriesIdAccessor="seriesId"
        nameAccessor="name"
      />
      <XYChart.AreaSeries
        data={smoothData}
        xAxisId="x-axis"
        yAxisId="y-axis"
        x0Accessor="x"
        y0Accessor="y0"
        y1Accessor="y1"
        seriesIdAccessor="seriesId"
        nameAccessor="name"
        pointsDisplay="always"
        curve="smooth"
      />
      <XYChart.XAxis id="x-axis" type="numerical" position="bottom" max={65} />
      <XYChart.YAxis id="y-axis" type="numerical" position="left" max={35} />
    </XYChart>
  );
};
```


#### Gap policy

The AreaSeries respects the `gapPolicy` set on a numerical or time axis. For
time axes, gap policy defaults to `gap`. Three options are available:

- `connect`: interpolates across the gap, connecting consecutive data points
regardless of the distance between them. This is the default behavior for
numerical axes.

- `gap`: leaves the gap visible as a break in the area. This option is only
available for time axes, where it is the default behavior.

- `threshold`: defines a maximum distance between consecutive data points. When
the distance is within the threshold, data points are connected; otherwise, a
gap is displayed. For time axes, the threshold is specified as a duration
string (e.g., `'3h'`, `'1d'`). For numerical axes, the threshold is a number
representing the maximum allowed distance between consecutive values.

For numerical axes, `gapPolicy` defaults to `connect`. Use the `threshold`
option to introduce gaps when the distance between data points exceeds a
specified value.

```tsx
import { XYChart } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';
import { Flex } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/layouts';

const dataWithGaps = [
  { x: '2024-01-01T00:00:00Z', y: 40, seriesId: 'cpu', name: 'CPU Usage' },
  { x: '2024-01-01T01:00:00Z', y: 55, seriesId: 'cpu', name: 'CPU Usage' },
  { x: '2024-01-01T02:00:00Z', y: 60, seriesId: 'cpu', name: 'CPU Usage' },
  { x: '2024-01-01T04:00:00Z', y: 45, seriesId: 'cpu', name: 'CPU Usage' },
  { x: '2024-01-01T05:00:00Z', y: 50, seriesId: 'cpu', name: 'CPU Usage' },
  { x: '2024-01-01T10:00:00Z', y: 70, seriesId: 'cpu', name: 'CPU Usage' },
  { x: '2024-01-01T11:00:00Z', y: 85, seriesId: 'cpu', name: 'CPU Usage' },
  { x: '2024-01-01T12:00:00Z', y: 90, seriesId: 'cpu', name: 'CPU Usage' },
  { x: '2024-01-01T14:00:00Z', y: 75, seriesId: 'cpu', name: 'CPU Usage' },
  { x: '2024-01-01T15:00:00Z', y: 65, seriesId: 'cpu', name: 'CPU Usage' },
];

const GapPolicy = () => {
  return (
    <Flex flexWrap="wrap">
      <Flex flexItem width="32%">
        <h5>Gap policy: connect</h5>
        <XYChart data={[]} height="200px">
          <XYChart.AreaSeries
            data={dataWithGaps}
            xAxisId="x-axis"
            yAxisId="y-axis"
            x0Accessor="x"
            y1Accessor="y"
            seriesIdAccessor="seriesId"
            nameAccessor="name"
          />
          <XYChart.XAxis
            id="x-axis"
            type="time"
            position="bottom"
            gapPolicy="connect"
          />
          <XYChart.YAxis id="y-axis" type="numerical" position="left" />
        </XYChart>
      </Flex>
      <Flex flexItem width="32%">
        <h5>Gap policy: gap (default for time)</h5>
        <XYChart data={[]} height="200px">
          <XYChart.AreaSeries
            data={dataWithGaps}
            xAxisId="x-axis"
            yAxisId="y-axis"
            x0Accessor="x"
            y1Accessor="y"
            seriesIdAccessor="seriesId"
            nameAccessor="name"
          />
          <XYChart.XAxis
            id="x-axis"
            type="time"
            position="bottom"
            gapPolicy="gap"
          />
          <XYChart.YAxis id="y-axis" type="numerical" position="left" />
        </XYChart>
      </Flex>
      <Flex flexItem width="32%">
        <h5>Gap policy: threshold (3h)</h5>
        <XYChart data={[]} height="200px">
          <XYChart.AreaSeries
            data={dataWithGaps}
            xAxisId="x-axis"
            yAxisId="y-axis"
            x0Accessor="x"
            y1Accessor="y"
            seriesIdAccessor="seriesId"
            nameAccessor="name"
          />
          <XYChart.XAxis
            id="x-axis"
            type="time"
            position="bottom"
            gapPolicy={{ threshold: '3h' }}
          />
          <XYChart.YAxis id="y-axis" type="numerical" position="left" />
        </XYChart>
      </Flex>
    </Flex>
  );
};
```

```tsx
import { Flex } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/layouts';

const dataWithGaps = [
  { x: '2024-01-01T00:00:00Z', y: 40, seriesId: 'cpu', name: 'CPU Usage' },
  { x: '2024-01-01T01:00:00Z', y: 55, seriesId: 'cpu', name: 'CPU Usage' },
```

#### Series actions

A series action is a creator-defined interaction with a given data point in the
chart. Basic interactions include copying a series name and inspecting the
underlying data of a data point. Series actions support both synchronous and
asynchronous callbacks. In order to enable chart interactions, the
`ChartSeriesAction` subcomponent needs to be appended within the `XYChart`. More
subcomponents can be added within this component, for instance
`ChartSeriesAction.Item`, where you can provide a custom action that will appear
in the legend menu. That action can execute any custom logic in its `onSelect`
callback or get disabled via a `disabled` prop. `Intents` could be added as part
of the series action as well with `ChartSeriesAction.Intent`.

If the datapoint belongs to a stack that has a categorical primary axis, the
datapoint together with the other stacked datapoints in the same category will
be returned.

CodeSandbox Still have questions?Find answers in the Dynatrace Community
- Import
- Overview
- Area vs Band
- Usage

- Data
- Custom Tooltip

- Value representation
- Series curve
- Gap policy
- Series actions

```tsx
import {
  useMemo,
  useState,
  type Dispatch,
  type ReactElement,
  type SetStateAction,
} from 'react';

import {
  XYChart,
  ChartSeriesAction,
  type SeriesActionsTemplate,
  type XYChartAreaSeriesActionItem,
} from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';
import { SimpleTable } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/tables';
import { MagnifyingGlassIcon } from '@dynatrace/strato-icons';

const categoricalData = [
  { cat: 'Q1', y: 30, seriesId: 'frontend', name: 'Frontend' },
  { cat: 'Q2', y: 45, seriesId: 'frontend', name: 'Frontend' },
  { cat: 'Q3', y: 41, seriesId: 'frontend', name: 'Frontend' },
  { cat: 'Q4', y: 47, seriesId: 'frontend', name: 'Frontend' },
  { cat: 'Q1', y: 20, seriesId: 'backend', name: 'Backend' },
  { cat: 'Q2', y: 35, seriesId: 'backend', name: 'Backend' },
  { cat: 'Q3', y: 28, seriesId: 'backend', name: 'Backend' },
  { cat: 'Q4', y: 32, seriesId: 'backend', name: 'Backend' },
  { cat: 'Q1', y: 10, seriesId: 'database', name: 'Database' },
  { cat: 'Q2', y: 15, seriesId: 'database', name: 'Database' },
  { cat: 'Q3', y: 12, seriesId: 'database', name: 'Database' },
  { cat: 'Q4', y: 18, seriesId: 'database', name: 'Database' },
];

const chartSeriesAction = (
  action: XYChartAreaSeriesActionItem,
  setter: Dispatch<SetStateAction<XYChartAreaSeriesActionItem | undefined>>
): ReactElement => {
  return (
    <ChartSeriesAction>
      <ChartSeriesAction.Item onSelect={() => setter(action)}>
        <ChartSeriesAction.ItemIcon>
          <MagnifyingGlassIcon />
        </ChartSeriesAction.ItemIcon>
        Inspect data point
      </ChartSeriesAction.Item>
    </ChartSeriesAction>
  );
};

const handleSeriesActions = (
  datapoint: XYChartAreaSeriesActionItem,
  setter: Dispatch<SetStateAction<XYChartAreaSeriesActionItem | undefined>>
): SeriesActionsTemplate => {
  return chartSeriesAction(datapoint, setter);
};

const columns = [
  { id: 'key', header: 'Key', accessor: 'key' },
  { id: 'value', header: 'Value', accessor: 'value' },
];

const SeriesActions = () => {
  const [selectedArea, setSelectedArea] =
    useState<XYChartAreaSeriesActionItem>();

  const tableData = useMemo(() => {
    if (selectedArea) {
      if (selectedArea.datapoints && selectedArea.datapoints.length > 0) {
        return selectedArea.datapoints.map((datapoint) => {
          const { x0, x1, y0, y1, ...rest } = datapoint;
          return [
            { key: 'x0', value: x0 },
            { key: 'x1', value: x1 },
            { key: 'y0', value: y0 },
            { key: 'y1', value: y1 },
            { key: 'Rest', value: JSON.stringify(rest) },
          ];
        });
      }

      const { x0, x1, y0, y1, name, ...rest } = selectedArea.datapoint;
      return [
        [
          { key: 'x0', value: x0 },
          { key: 'x1', value: x1 },
          { key: 'y0', value: y0 },
          { key: 'y1', value: y1 },
          { key: 'name', value: name },
          { key: 'Rest', value: JSON.stringify(rest) },
        ],
      ];
    }
    return [];
  }, [selectedArea]);

  return (
    <>
      <XYChart data={[]} colorPalette="categorical">
        <XYChart.AreaSeries
          data={categoricalData}
          xAxisId="x-axis"
          yAxisId="y-axis"
          x0Accessor="cat"
          y1Accessor="y"
          seriesIdAccessor="seriesId"
          nameAccessor="name"
          actions={(datapoint) =>
            handleSeriesActions(datapoint, setSelectedArea)
          }
        />
        <XYChart.XAxis id="x-axis" type="categorical" position="bottom" />
        <XYChart.YAxis id="y-axis" type="numerical" position="left" />
      </XYChart>

      {selectedArea && (
        <div style={{ marginTop: '20px' }}>
          <h4>Selected Series</h4>
          <div style={{ display: 'flex', gap: '20px', flexWrap: 'wrap' }}>
            {tableData.map((td, index) => (
              <div key={index} style={{ marginBottom: '20px' }}>
                <h5>Datapoint {index + 1}</h5>
                <SimpleTable columns={columns} data={td} />
              </div>
            ))}
          </div>
        </div>
      )}
    </>
  );
};
```

```tsx
};

const columns = [
  { id: 'key', header: 'Key', accessor: 'key' },
  { id: 'value', header: 'Value', accessor: 'value' },
];

const SeriesActions = () => {

    useState<XYChartAreaSeriesActionItem>();

  const tableData = useMemo(() => {
    if (selectedArea) {
      if (selectedArea.datapoints && selectedArea.datapoints.length > 0) {
        return selectedArea.datapoints.map((datapoint) => {

            { key: 'x0', value: x0 },
            { key: 'x1', value: x1 },
            { key: 'y0', value: y0 },
            { key: 'y1', value: y1 },
            { key: 'Rest', value: JSON.stringify(rest) },
          ];
        });
      }

      const { x0, x1, y0, y1, name, ...rest } = selectedArea.datapoint;
      return [
        [
          { key: 'x0', value: x0 },
          { key: 'x1', value: x1 },
          { key: 'y0', value: y0 },
          { key: 'y1', value: y1 },
          { key: 'name', value: name },
          { key: 'Rest', value: JSON.stringify(rest) },
        ],
      ];
    }
    return [];
  }, [selectedArea]);

  return (
    <>
      <XYChart data={[]} colorPalette="categorical">
        <XYChart.AreaSeries
          data={categoricalData}
          xAxisId="x-axis"
          yAxisId="y-axis"
          x0Accessor="cat"
          y1Accessor="y"
          seriesIdAccessor="seriesId"
          nameAccessor="name"
          actions={(datapoint) =>
            handleSeriesActions(datapoint, setSelectedArea)
          }
        />
        <XYChart.XAxis id="x-axis" type="categorical" position="bottom" />
        <XYChart.YAxis id="y-axis" type="numerical" position="left" />
      </XYChart>

      {selectedArea && (
        <div style={{ marginTop: '20px' }}>
          <h4>Selected Series</h4>
          <div style={{ display: 'flex', gap: '20px', flexWrap: 'wrap' }}>
            {tableData.map((td, index) => (
              <div key={index} style={{ marginBottom: '20px' }}>
                <h5>Datapoint {index + 1}</h5>

              </div>
            ))}
          </div>
        </div>
      )}
    </>
  );
};
```


### Props

- XYChart-AreaSeries
## AreaSeries
An area series chart fills the region between data points and a baseline, making
it effective for visualizing magnitudes, trends, and cumulative values over a
continuous range. It combines line-based trend visibility with filled regions to
emphasize volume, helping to reveal patterns and comparisons across series.

OverviewProperties

### XYChart.AreaSeries

`XYChart.AreaSeries` provides a slot for defining an `AreaSeries`.

#### XYChartAreaSeriesProps

##### Signature:
`export declare type Props = & & {
 x0Accessor?: ;
 y0Accessor?: ;
 x1Accessor?: ;
 y1Accessor?: ;
 seriesIdAccessor: ;
 nameAccessor: ;
 /**
 * Show the datapoints always, or auto based on a threshold amount.
 * @defaultValue
 */
 pointsDisplay?: ;
 /**
 * Area interpolation curve type
 */
 curve?: ;
 /**
 * Custom actions to be shown in the legend.
 * (Upcoming for tooltip)
 * For legend, the first parameter returns the first datapoint of the series and the second parameter returns the series object.
 */
 actions?: (datapoint: , series?: ) => ;
};`Still have questions?Find answers in the Dynatrace Community
- XYChart.AreaSeries

---

## XYChart-BarSeries

`/design/data-visualizations/charts/XYChart-BarSeries/`

## BarSeries
The BarSeries renders rectangular bars to visualize values across categories,
numbers or time. It supports simple, grouped (clustered), and stacked layouts,
making it ideal for comparisons, totals, and distributions. The BarSeries works
with numeric, time, and categorical axes, and handles a wide range of data
types—perfect for Bar, Column, and Stacked charts.

OverviewProperties

### Import

`tsx
import { XYChart } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';
`

### Overview

To better understand the accessor patterns, here are some common terms used in
this documentation:

- Primary Accessor: Defines the bar's position along the main axis (e.g.,
`x0Accessor` for vertical bars).

- Growth Accessor: Defines the bar's length or height (e.g., `y1Accessor`
for vertical bars).

- Primary Axis: The axis associated with the primary accessor.

- Growth Axis: The axis associated with the growth accessor.

- Base Bucket: A discrete slot or category on the primary axis where one or
more bars are drawn. For a categorical axis, each category is a bucket.

A Bar series displays values as rectangular bars spreading from a configurable
base X or Y axis. It supports three variants: single, stacked, and grouped, and
works with categorical, numerical, time, and log axes.

- Single: one bar per base bucket, shown as a vertical or horizontal bar.

- Stacked: bars from multiple series accumulate along the growth axis. Supported
only when the growth accessor is numerical, time, or log.

- Grouped: multiple series appear side-by-side within each base bucket for
direct comparison. Supported only when the primary accessor is categorical.

- Range bars: a bar that spans between two values. Available in the grouped and
single variants.

- Full rectangles: manual placement using four accessors (`x0Accessor`,
`x1Accessor`, `y0Accessor`, `y1Accessor`) to draw arbitrary rectangles; useful
for waterfall-like layouts (single variant only).

```tsx
import {
  XYChart,
  type BarDatapoint,
} from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';

const data = (() => {
  const data: BarDatapoint[] = [];
  const mean = 100;
  const stdDev = 30;

  // Function to approximate normal distribution
  const normalDist = (x: number) => {
    return Math.exp(-((x - mean) ** 2) / (2 * stdDev ** 2));
  };

  for (let x0 = 0; x0 < 200; x0 += 5) {
    const x1 = x0 + 5;
    const midpoint = (x0 + x1) / 2;
    const y1 = Math.round(normalDist(midpoint) * 100);

    data.push({
      x0,
      x1,
      y1,
      seriesId: 'series-normal',
      name: 'Normal Distribution',
    });
  }

  return data;
})();

const BarSeriesSimpleVertical = () => {
  return (
    <>
      <h3>Simple vertical bar</h3>
      <XYChart data={[]}>
        <XYChart.BarSeries
          data={data}
          variant="single"
          x0Accessor="x0"
          x1Accessor="x1"
          y1Accessor="y1"
          seriesIdAccessor="seriesId"
          nameAccessor="name"
          yAxisId="y-axis"
          xAxisId="x-axis"
        />
        <XYChart.XAxis id="x-axis" type="numerical" position="bottom" />
        <XYChart.YAxis id="y-axis" type="numerical" position="left" />
      </XYChart>
    </>
  );
};
```

```tsx
import {
  XYChart,
  type BarDatapoint,
} from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';

const data = (() => {
  const data: BarDatapoint[] = [];
  const mean = 100;
  const stdDev = 30;

  // Function to approximate normal distribution
  const normalDist = (x: number) => {
    return Math.exp(-((x - mean) ** 2) / (2 * stdDev ** 2));
  };

  for (let x0 = 0; x0 < 200; x0 += 5) {
    const x1 = x0 + 5;
    const midpoint = (x0 + x1) / 2;
    const y1 = Math.round(normalDist(midpoint) * 100);

    data.push({
      x0,
      x1,
      y1,
      seriesId: 'series-normal',
      name: 'Normal Distribution',
    });
  }

  return data;
})();

const BarSeriesSimpleVertical = () => {
  return (
    <>
      <h3>Simple vertical bar</h3>
      <XYChart data={[]}>
        <XYChart.BarSeries
          data={data}
          variant="single"
          x0Accessor="x0"
          x1Accessor="x1"
          y1Accessor="y1"
          seriesIdAccessor="seriesId"
          nameAccessor="name"
          yAxisId="y-axis"
          xAxisId="x-axis"
        />
        <XYChart.XAxis id="x-axis" type="numerical" position="bottom" />
        <XYChart.YAxis id="y-axis" type="numerical" position="left" />
      </XYChart>
    </>
  );
};
```


### Usage

Add `XYChart.BarSeries` inside `XYChart`. Provide accessors to describe the
bar’s position (primary accessor) and growth (growth accessor). Orientation is
inferred from the accessor pattern, no orientation prop required.

Required accessors depend on the pattern:

- Vertical single bars: `x0Accessor` (position) + `y1Accessor` (height)

- Horizontal single bars: `y0Accessor` (position) + `x1Accessor` (width)

- Vertical floating bars: `x0Accessor` + `y0Accessor` + `y1Accessor`

- Horizontal floating bars: `y0Accessor` + `x0Accessor` + `x1Accessor`

- Vertical range bars: `x0Accessor` + `x1Accessor` + `y1Accessor`

- Horizontal range bars: `y0Accessor` + `y1Accessor` + `x1Accessor`

- Full rectangle (manual placement): `x0Accessor` + `x1Accessor` +
`y0Accessor` + `y1Accessor` (single variant only)

Additionally:

- `seriesIdAccessor`: groups datapoints into series (enables multiple series in
one BarSeries slot)

- `nameAccessor`: series label for legends and tooltips

Assign the series to axes via `xAxisId` and `yAxisId`. See
XYChart docs for axis configuration.

### Variants

#### Single

This type refers to independent bars, which support all valid accessor patterns.
Specific details for the accessors structure can be found below:

- Automatic direction:

- Vertical bars: `x0Accessor + y1Accessor`

- Horizontal bars: `y0Accessor + x1Accessor`

- Waterfall-like rectangles:

- Use `variant="single"` with
`x0Accessor + x1Accessor + y0Accessor + y1Accessor` to manually position
rectangles. Stacking and grouping are not supported for full rectangles.

- Note:

- Multiple floating range bars per category can overlap in `variant="single"`.
Use `variant="group"` with `x0Accessor + y0Accessor + y1Accessor` to show
them side-by-side.

Examples:

```tsx
import {
  XYChart,
  type BarDatapoint,
} from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';

const generateNormalDistributionData = (centerpoint: number = 100) => {
  const data: BarDatapoint[] = [];
  const mean = centerpoint;
  const stdDev = 30;

  const normalDist = (x: number) => {
    return Math.exp(-((x - mean) ** 2) / (2 * stdDev ** 2));
  };

  for (let x0 = 0; x0 < 200; x0 += 5) {
    const x1 = x0 + 5;
    const midpoint = (x0 + x1) / 2;
    const y1 = Math.round(normalDist(midpoint) * 100);

    data.push({
      x0,
      x1,
      y1,
      seriesId: 'centered on ' + centerpoint,
      name: 'Normal Distribution centered on ' + centerpoint,
    });
  }

  return data;
};

// One floating bar per category to avoid overlap in variant="single"
const floatingBarsSingle: BarDatapoint[] = [
  {
    x0: 'sales',
    y0: new Date('2023-01-02'),
    y1: new Date('2023-01-06'),
    seriesId: 'Task',
    name: 'Task',
  },
  {
    x0: 'marketing',
    y0: new Date('2023-01-07'),
    y1: new Date('2023-01-11'),
    seriesId: 'Task',
    name: 'Task',
  },
  {
    x0: 'law',
    y0: new Date('2023-01-04'),
    y1: new Date('2023-01-08'),
    seriesId: 'Task',
    name: 'Task',
  },
];

const BarSeriesVerticalSingle = () => {
  return (
    <div style={{ display: 'flex', gap: '2rem', flexWrap: 'wrap' }}>
      <div style={{ flex: '1', minWidth: '400px' }}>
        <h3>Normal Distribution</h3>
        <XYChart data={[]}>
          <XYChart.BarSeries
            data={[
              ...generateNormalDistributionData(40),
              ...generateNormalDistributionData(100),
              ...generateNormalDistributionData(160),
            ]}
            variant="single"
            x0Accessor="x0"
            x1Accessor="x1"
            y1Accessor="y1"
            seriesIdAccessor="seriesId"
            nameAccessor="name"
            yAxisId="y-axis"
            xAxisId="x-axis"
          />
          <XYChart.XAxis id="x-axis" type="numerical" position="bottom" />
          <XYChart.YAxis id="y-axis" type="numerical" position="left" />
          <XYChart.Legend hidden />
        </XYChart>
      </div>

      <div style={{ flex: '1', minWidth: '400px' }}>
        <h3>Floating Bars</h3>
        <XYChart data={[{}]} colorPalette="categorical">
          <XYChart.BarSeries
            data={floatingBarsSingle}
            variant="single"
            x0Accessor="x0"
            y0Accessor="y0"
            y1Accessor="y1"
            seriesIdAccessor="seriesId"
            nameAccessor="name"
            xAxisId="x-axis"
            yAxisId="y-axis"
          />
          <XYChart.XAxis id="x-axis" type="categorical" position="bottom" />
          <XYChart.YAxis id="y-axis" type="time" position="left" />
          <XYChart.Legend hidden />
        </XYChart>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
};
```

```tsx
import {
  XYChart,
  type BarDatapoint,
} from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';

const generateNormalDistributionData = (centerpoint: number = 100) => {
  const data: BarDatapoint[] = [];
  const mean = centerpoint;
  const stdDev = 30;

  const normalDist = (x: number) => {
    return Math.exp(-((x - mean) ** 2) / (2 * stdDev ** 2));
  };

  for (let x0 = 0; x0 < 200; x0 += 5) {
    const x1 = x0 + 5;
    const midpoint = (x0 + x1) / 2;
    const y1 = Math.round(normalDist(midpoint) * 100);

    data.push({
      x0,
      x1,
      y1,
      seriesId: 'centered on ' + centerpoint,
      name: 'Normal Distribution centered on ' + centerpoint,
    });
  }

  return data;
};

// One floating bar per category to avoid overlap in variant="single"
const floatingBarsSingle: BarDatapoint[] = [
  {
    x0: 'sales',

```


#### Stack

Data can be displayed stacked by setting the BarSeries `variant` to `stack`.
Bars stack at the same primary accessor by using the `seriesIdAccessor` to group
the data. Stack behaviour is supported for all axes except categorical-type if
they belong to a growth axis.

These accessors patterns are valid for stacking:

- Vertical stacked bars: `x0Accessor + y1Accessor`

- Horizontal stacked bars: `y0Accessor + x1Accessor`

- Stacked bars with X-range span: `x0Accessor + x1Accessor + y1Accessor` (stacks
vertically, grouped by the X range)

- Stacked bars with Y-range span: `y0Accessor + y1Accessor + x1Accessor` (stacks
horizontally, grouped by the Y range)

The following accessor patterns are not supported for stacking:

- Floating range bars (Y-range only: `y0Accessor + y1Accessor`)

- Full rectangles (`x0Accessor + x1Accessor + y0Accessor + y1Accessor`)

Examples:

```tsx
import {
  XYChart,
  type BarDatapoint,
} from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';

const generateNormalDistributionDataYRange = (centerpoint: number = 100) => {
  const data: BarDatapoint[] = [];
  const mean = centerpoint;
  const stdDev = 30;

  const normalDist = (y: number) => {
    return Math.exp(-((y - mean) ** 2) / (2 * stdDev ** 2));
  };

  for (let y0 = 0; y0 < 200; y0 += 5) {
    const y1 = y0 + 5;
    const midpoint = (y0 + y1) / 2;
    const x1 = Math.round(normalDist(midpoint) * 100);

    data.push({
      y0,
      y1,
      x1,
      seriesId: 'centered on ' + centerpoint,
      name: 'Normal Distribution centered on ' + centerpoint,
    });
  }

  return data;
};

const categoricalHorizontalStack = [
  { y0: 'Mobile App', seriesId: 'Unacceptable', x1: 8, name: 'Unacceptable' },
  { y0: 'Mobile App', seriesId: 'Poor', x1: 21, name: 'Poor' },
  { y0: 'Mobile App', seriesId: 'Fair', x1: 45, name: 'Fair' },
  { y0: 'Mobile App', seriesId: 'Good', x1: 212, name: 'Good' },
  { y0: 'Mobile App', seriesId: 'Excellent', x1: 32, name: 'Excellent' },

  {
    y0: 'Marketing Web',
    seriesId: 'Unacceptable',
    x1: 3,
    name: 'Unacceptable',
  },
  { y0: 'Marketing Web', seriesId: 'Poor', x1: 0, name: 'Poor' },
  { y0: 'Marketing Web', seriesId: 'Fair', x1: 25, name: 'Fair' },
  { y0: 'Marketing Web', seriesId: 'Good', x1: 154, name: 'Good' },
  { y0: 'Marketing Web', seriesId: 'Excellent', x1: 78, name: 'Excellent' },

  {
    y0: 'Social Media',
    seriesId: 'Unacceptable',
    x1: 11,
    name: 'Unacceptable',
  },
  { y0: 'Social Media', seriesId: 'Poor', x1: 32, name: 'Poor' },
  { y0: 'Social Media', seriesId: 'Fair', x1: 45, name: 'Fair' },
  { y0: 'Social Media', seriesId: 'Good', x1: 212, name: 'Good' },
  { y0: 'Social Media', seriesId: 'Excellent', x1: 57, name: 'Excellent' },

  { y0: 'E-commerce', seriesId: 'Unacceptable', x1: 0, name: 'Unacceptable' },
  { y0: 'E-commerce', seriesId: 'Poor', x1: 12, name: 'Poor' },
  { y0: 'E-commerce', seriesId: 'Fair', x1: 4, name: 'Fair' },
  { y0: 'E-commerce', seriesId: 'Good', x1: 26, name: 'Good' },
  { y0: 'E-commerce', seriesId: 'Excellent', x1: 253, name: 'Excellent' },
];

const BarSeriesStackHorizontal = () => {
  return (
    <div style={{ display: 'flex', gap: '2rem', flexWrap: 'wrap' }}>
      <div style={{ flex: '1', minWidth: '400px' }}>
        <h3>Horizontal Stacked Bars (Categorical)</h3>
        <XYChart data={categoricalHorizontalStack} colorPalette="fireplace">
          <XYChart.BarSeries
            variant="stack"
            y0Accessor="y0" // primary position (categorical)
            x1Accessor="x1" // growth (numerical)
            seriesIdAccessor="seriesId"
            nameAccessor="name"
            xAxisId="x-axis"
            yAxisId="y-axis"
          />
          <XYChart.XAxis id="x-axis" type="numerical" position="bottom" />
          <XYChart.YAxis id="y-axis" type="categorical" position="left" />
          <XYChart.Legend hidden />
        </XYChart>
      </div>

      <div style={{ flex: '1', minWidth: '400px' }}>
        <h3>Horizontal Stack with Y-range span</h3>
        <XYChart data={[]}>
          <XYChart.BarSeries
            data={[
              ...generateNormalDistributionDataYRange(40),
              ...generateNormalDistributionDataYRange(100),
              ...generateNormalDistributionDataYRange(160),
            ]}
            variant="stack"
            y0Accessor="y0" // start of Y range (numerical)
            y1Accessor="y1" // end of Y range (numerical)
            x1Accessor="x1" // growth along X (numerical)
            seriesIdAccessor="seriesId"
            nameAccessor="name"
            xAxisId="x-axis"
            yAxisId="y-axis"
          />
          <XYChart.XAxis id="x-axis" type="numerical" position="bottom" />
          <XYChart.YAxis id="y-axis" type="numerical" position="left" />
          <XYChart.Legend hidden />
        </XYChart>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
};
```

```tsx
import {

} from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';

const generateNormalDistributionDataYRange = (centerpoint: number = 100) => {
  const data: BarDatapoint[] = [];
  const mean = centerpoint;

    return Math.exp(-((y - mean) ** 2) / (2 * stdDev ** 2));
  };

      y0,
      y1,

};

const categoricalHorizontalStack = [
  { y0: 'Mobile App', seriesId: 'Unacceptable', x1: 8, name: 'Unacceptable' },
  { y0: 'Mobile App', seriesId: 'Poor', x1: 21, name: 'Poor' },

  },

  { y0: 'Marketing Web', seriesId: 'Fair', x1: 25, name: 'Fair' },
  { y0: 'Marketing Web', seriesId: 'Good', x1: 154, name: 'Good' },
  { y0: 'Marketing Web', seriesId: 'Excellent', x1: 78, name: 'Excellent' },

  {
    y0: 'Social Media',
    seriesId: 'Unacceptable',
    x1: 11,
    name: 'Unacceptable',
  },
  { y0: 'Social Media', seriesId: 'Poor', x1: 32, name: 'Poor' },
  { y0: 'Social Media', seriesId: 'Fair', x1: 45, name: 'Fair' },

  { y0: 'E-commerce', seriesId: 'Poor', x1: 12, name: 'Poor' },
  { y0: 'E-commerce', seriesId: 'Fair', x1: 4, name: 'Fair' },

const BarSeriesStackHorizontal = () => {

        <h3>Horizontal Stacked Bars (Categorical)</h3>
        <XYChart data={categoricalHorizontalStack} colorPalette="fireplace">
          <XYChart.BarSeries
            variant="stack"
            y0Accessor="y0" // primary position (categorical)

            y1Accessor="y1" // end of Y range (numerical)

          <XYChart.YAxis id="y-axis" type="numerical" position="left" />

};
```

#### Group

Data can also be displayed grouped by setting the BarSeries `variant` to
`group`, where the bars will be positioned side-by-side within the same bucket.
In order to group, a categorical primary axis is required (the axis used for
`x0Accessor` in vertical bars or `y0Accessor` in horizontal bars). If the
primary axis is numerical, time, or log (i.e., not categorical), the series will
fall back to `variant="single"`.

These accessors patterns are valid for grouping:

- Vertical grouped bars: `x0Accessor + y1Accessor`

- Vertical grouped range bars: `x0Accessor + y0Accessor + y1Accessor`

- Horizontal grouped bars: `y0Accessor + x1Accessor`

- Horizontal grouped range bars: `y0Accessor + x0Accessor + x1Accessor`

The following accessor patterns are not supported for grouping:

- X-range with growth (`x0Accessor + x1Accessor + y1Accessor`)

- Y-range with growth (`y0Accessor + y1Accessor + x1Accessor`)

- Full rectangles (`x0Accessor + x1Accessor + y0Accessor + y1Accessor`)

Examples:

```tsx
import { XYChart } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';

const multipleAppRatingPositiveFlat = [
  { x0: 'mobile-app', series: 'Unacceptable', y1: 8 },
  { x0: 'mobile-app', series: 'Poor', y1: 21 },
  { x0: 'mobile-app', series: 'Fair', y1: 45 },
  { x0: 'mobile-app', series: 'Good', y1: 212 },
  { x0: 'mobile-app', series: 'Excellent', y1: 32 },
  {
    x0: 'marketing-web-app-dynatrace-cool-app-here',
    series: 'Unacceptable',
    y1: 3,
  },
  { x0: 'marketing-web-app-dynatrace-cool-app-here', series: 'Poor', y1: 0 },
  { x0: 'marketing-web-app-dynatrace-cool-app-here', series: 'Fair', y1: 25 },
  { x0: 'marketing-web-app-dynatrace-cool-app-here', series: 'Good', y1: 154 },
  {
    x0: 'marketing-web-app-dynatrace-cool-app-here',
    series: 'Excellent',
    y1: 78,
  },
  { x0: 'social-media-app', series: 'Unacceptable', y1: 11 },
  { x0: 'social-media-app', series: 'Poor', y1: 32 },
  { x0: 'social-media-app', series: 'Fair', y1: 45 },
  { x0: 'social-media-app', series: 'Good', y1: 212 },
  { x0: 'social-media-app', series: 'Excellent', y1: 57 },
  { x0: 'ecommerce-web-app', series: 'Unacceptable', y1: 0 },
  { x0: 'ecommerce-web-app', series: 'Poor', y1: 12 },
  { x0: 'ecommerce-web-app', series: 'Fair', y1: 4 },
  { x0: 'ecommerce-web-app', series: 'Good', y1: 26 },
  { x0: 'ecommerce-web-app', series: 'Excellent', y1: 253 },
];

const stackTime = [
  {
    d: {
      host: 'sales',
      start: new Date('2023-01-01'),
      end: new Date('2023-01-05'),
    },
    seriesId: 'First Employee',

  },
  {

      host: 'sales',
      start: new Date('2023-01-04'),
      end: new Date('2023-01-06'),
    },
    seriesId: 'Third Employee',
  },
  {
    d: {
      host: 'marketing',
      start: new Date('2023-01-07'),
      end: new Date('2023-01-10'),
    },
    seriesId: 'First Employee',
  },
  {
    d: {

    },

  },
  {

      end: new Date('2023-01-05'),
    },
    seriesId: 'Third Employee',
  },
  {
    d: {
      host: 'law',
      start: new Date('2023-01-05'),
      end: new Date('2023-01-09'),
    },
    seriesId: 'Forth Employee',
  },
  {
    d: {
      host: 'marketing',
      start: new Date('2023-01-09'),
      end: new Date('2023-01-13'),
    },
    seriesId: 'Second Employee',
  },
  {
    d: {
      host: 'sales',
      start: new Date('2023-01-06'),
      end: new Date('2023-01-11'),
    },
    seriesId: 'Forth Employee',
  },
  {
    d: {
      host: 'marketing',
      start: new Date('2023-01-10'),
      end: new Date('2023-01-13'),
    },
    seriesId: 'Third Employee',
  },
  {
    d: {
      host: 'sales',
      start: new Date('2023-01-08'),
      end: new Date('2023-01-09'),
    },
    seriesId: 'Fifth Employee',
  },
];

const BarSeriesGroupVertical = () => {
  return (
    <div style={{ display: 'flex', gap: '2rem', flexWrap: 'wrap' }}>
      <div style={{ flex: '1', minWidth: '400px' }}>
        <XYChart data={[]}>
          <XYChart.BarSeries
            data={multipleAppRatingPositiveFlat}
            variant="group"
            x0Accessor="x0"
            y1Accessor="y1"
            seriesIdAccessor="series"
            nameAccessor="series"
            yAxisId="y-axis"
            xAxisId="x-axis"
          />
          <XYChart.XAxis id="x-axis" type="categorical" position="bottom" />
          <XYChart.YAxis id="y-axis" type="numerical" position="left" />
          <XYChart.Legend hidden />
        </XYChart>
      </div>
      <div style={{ flex: '1', minWidth: '400px' }}>
        <XYChart data={stackTime} colorPalette="fireplace">
          <XYChart.BarSeries
            variant="group"
            x0Accessor="d.host"
            y0Accessor="d.start"
            y1Accessor="d.end"
            seriesIdAccessor="seriesId"
            nameAccessor="seriesId"
            xAxisId="x-axis"
            yAxisId="y-axis"
          />
          <XYChart.XAxis id="x-axis" type="categorical" position="bottom" />
          <XYChart.YAxis id="y-axis" type="time" position="left" />
          <XYChart.Legend hidden />
        </XYChart>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
};
```

### Axis compatibility

The rendering behavior of `BarSeries` variants is tightly coupled with the axis
types they are assigned to. To ensure the chart renders as expected, please note
the following requirements:

- Grouping requires a categorical primary axis (e.g., categorical X-axis for
vertical bars).

- Stacking works with numerical, time, and log axes. A categorical growth axis
cannot stack.

- Unsupported combinations automatically fall back to supported behavior,
typically `variant="single"`, and log a warning.

### Data

The data provided to the `BarSeries` component via the `data` property will be
used in combination with the accessors of each series.

#### Coloring

By using the `color` and `colorPalette` properties in the `BarSeries` you can
overwrite the coloring from the `XYChart` component. These two properties are
mutually exclusive. For more information about how to use coloring in the chart
you can refer to the XYChart docs.

The `XYChart.BarSeries` subcomponent supports functions for the `color` and
`colorPalette` props to customize coloring per series in case more than one
series is presented in the same slot.

```tsx
import { XYChart } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/charts';
import { Surface } from '@dynatrace/strato-components/layouts';

const verticalData = [
  { x0: 'Alpha', y1: 12, seriesId: 'Muted', name: 'Muted' },
  { x0: 'Alpha', y1: 22, seriesId: 'Low', name: 'Low' },
  { x0: 'Alpha', y1: 35, seriesId: 'Medium', name: 'Medium' },
  { x0: 'Alpha', y1: 60, seriesId: 'High', name: 'High' },
  { x0: 'Alpha', y1: 85, seriesId: 'Critical', name: 'Critical' },

  { x0: 'Beta', y1: 18, seriesId: 'Muted', name: 'Muted' },
  { x0: 'Beta', y1: 28, seriesId: 'Low', name: 'Low' },
  { x0: 'Beta', y1: 40, seriesId: 'Medium', name: 'Medium' },
  { x0: 'Beta', y1: 55, seriesId: 'High', name: 'High' },
  { x0: 'Beta', y1: 75, seriesId: 'Critical', name: 'Critical' },

  { x0: 'Gamma', y1: 9, seriesId: 'Muted', name: 'Muted' },
  { x0: 'Gamma', y1: 20, seriesId: 'Low', name: 'Low' },
  { x0: 'Gamma', y1: 32, seriesId: 'Medium', name: 'Medium' },
  { x0: 'Gamma', y1: 48, seriesId: 'High', name: 'High' },
  { x0: 'Gamma', y1: 70, seriesId: 'Critical', name: 'Critical' },

  { x0: 'Delta', y1: 15, seriesId: 'Muted', name: 'Muted' },
  { x0: 'Delta', y1: 26, seriesId: 'Low', name: 'Low' },
  { x0: 'Delta', y1: 38, seriesId: 'Medium', name: 'Medium' },
  { x0: 'Delta', y1: 52, seriesId: 'High', name: 'High' },
  { x0: 'Delta', y1: 80, seriesId: 'Critical', name: 'Critical' },
];

const horizontalData = [
  { y0: 'Alpha', x1: 12, seriesId: 'Muted', name: 'Muted' },
  { y0: 'Alpha', x1: 22, seriesId: 'Low', name: 'Low' },
  { y0: 'Alpha', x1: 35, seriesId: 'Medium', name: 'Medium' },
  { y0: 'Alpha', x1: 60, seriesId: 'High', name: 'High' },
  { y0: 'Alpha', x1: 85, seriesId: 'Critical', name: 'Critical' },

  { y0: 'Beta', x1: 18, seriesId: 'Muted', name: 'Muted' },
  { y0: 'Beta', x1: 28, seriesId: 'Low', name: 'Low' },
  { y0: 'Beta', x1: 40, seriesId: 'Medium', name: 'Medium' },
  { y0: 'Beta', x1: 55, seriesId: 'High', name: 'High' },
  { y0: 'Beta', x1: 75, seriesId: 'Critical', name: 'Critical' },

  { y0: 'Gamma', x1: 9, seriesId: 'Muted', name: 'Muted' },
  { y0: 'Gamma', x1: 20, seriesId: 'Low', name: 'Low' },
  { y0: 'Gamma', x1: 32, seriesId: 'Medium', name: 'Medium' },
  { y0: 'Gamma', x1: 48, seriesId: 'High', name: 'High' },
  { y0: 'Gamma', x1: 70, seriesId: 'Critical', name: 'Critical' },

  { y0: 'Delta', x1: 15, seriesId: 'Muted', name: 'Muted' },
  { y0: 'Delta', x1: 26, seriesId: 'Low', name: 'Low' },
  { y0: 'Delta', x1: 38, seriesId: 'Medium', name: 'Medium' },
  { y0: 'Delta', x1: 52, seriesId: 'High', name: 'High' },
  { y0: 'Delta', x1: 80, seriesId: 'Critical', name: 'Critical' },
];

const BarSeriesColor = () => {
  return (
    <div
      style={{
        display: 'grid',
        gridTemplateColumns: '1fr 1fr',
        gap: '1em',
      }}
    >
      <Surface>
        <XYChart data={[{}]} height="200px" colorPalette="security-risk-level">
          <XYChart.BarSeries
            data={verticalData}
            variant="stack"
            xAxisId="x-bottom-axis"
            yAxisId="y-left-axis"
            x0Accessor="x0" // base (categorical)
            y1Accessor="y1" // growth (numerical)
            nameAccessor="name"
            seriesIdAccessor="seriesId"
          />
          <XYChart.XAxis
            id="x-bottom-axis"
            type="categorical"
            position="bottom"
          />
          <XYChart.YAxis id="y-left-axis" type="numerical" position="left" />
        </XYChart>
      </Surface>

      <Surface>
        <XYChart data={[{}]} height="200px">
          <XYChart.BarSeries
            data={verticalData}
            variant="stack"
            xAxisId="x-bottom-axis"
            yAxisId="y-left-axis"
            x0Accessor="x0"
            y1Accessor="y1"
            nameAccessor="name"
            seriesIdAccessor="seriesId"
            color={({ seriesId }) =>
              seriesId === 'Critical'
                ? 'red'
                : seriesId === 'High'
                ? 'orange'
                : 'steelblue'
            }
          />
          <XYChart.XAxis
            id="x-bottom-axis"
            type="categorical"
            position="bottom"
          />
          <XYChart.YAxis id="y-left-axis" type="numerical" position="left" />
        </XYChart>
      </Surface>

      <Surface>
        <XYChart data={[{}]} height="200px" colorPalette="security-risk-level">
          <XYChart.BarSeries
            data={horizontalData}
            variant="stack"
            xAxisId="x-bottom-axis"
            yAxisId="y-left-axis"
            y0Accessor="y0" // base (categorical)
            x1Accessor="x1" // growth (numerical)
            nameAccessor="name"
            seriesIdAccessor="seriesId"
          />
          <XYChart.XAxis
            id="x-bottom-axis"
            type="numerical"
            position="bottom"
          />
          <XYChart.YAxis id="y-left-axis" type="categorical" position="left" />
        </XYChart>
      </Surface>

      <Surface>
        <XYChart data={[{}]} height="200px">
          <XYChart.BarSeries
            data={horizontalData}
            variant="stack"
            xAxisId="x-bottom-axis"
            yAxisId="y-left-axis"
            y0Accessor="y0"
            x1Accessor="x1"
            nameAccessor="name"
            seriesIdAccessor="seriesId"
            color={({ seriesId }) =>
              seriesId === 'Critical'
                ? 'red'
                : seriesId === 'High'
                ? 'orange'
                : 'steelblue'
            }
          />
          <XYChart.XAxis
            id="x-bottom-axis"
            type="numerical"
            position="bottom"
          />
          <XYChart.YAxis id="y-left-axis" type="categorical" position="left" />
        </XYChart>
      </Surface>
    </div>
  );
};
```
